文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The three alloys showed the same trend regarding their response to the strengthening process. As these alloys reached their highest strength with the T6 heat treatment, in using the T7 treatment, viz., increasing the aging temperature and time, they were overaged, so that the strength decreased and the ductility increased, as explained above in detail. For example, for alloy B, the UTS, YS, and %El values in the as-cast condition are 249.8 MPa, 166.9 MPa, and 3.7%, respectively. With T6 heat treatment, specifically S4WA2, the alloy achieved its highest strength with 373.7 MPa UTS, 304.2 MPa YS, and ductility of 2.7%. Whereas after the T7 heat treatments (S4WA3 and S4WA4), the alloy started to soften and exhibit lower strength values and higher ductility. At ambient temperature, and with solution heat treatment for 4 h, alloy B gave the highest strength values with the S4WA2 heat treatment.

The loading condition which is experienced by the material when loaded uniaxially is manifested in the boundary conditions applied to the unit cell model. 问题:Which compound has a yield strength of 200 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Due to their mechanical strength and ductility, metals are commonly used for endoprostheses, fixation of fractures and intravascular implants . CoCr alloys are employed as they combine outstanding yield strength with a moderate elastic modulus of about 200 MPa and rather low wear rates . At the same time, there is still considerable risk for the release of nanoparticles due to wear processes or toxic ions such as Co, Cr and Ni , which may lead to interference with several signalling pathways within the cell groups, sometimes up to apoptosis or necrosis . Furthermore, reactive chemical species as H2O2, O2− or NO released by activated macrophages can alter the alloy surface oxide composition , even for presumably inert CoCr alloys, thus showing that a biological and cellular environment can be as aggressive as gas turbine environments. However, the concomitant increase of the oxide thickness is supposed to explain the observed decrease in metal ion release shortly after device implantation in in-vivo tests . On the other hand, amino acids can react with CoCr alloys under tribological contact with the subsequent formation of a lubricating organometallic film at the interface, thus reducing the friction coefficient and the wear rate .
DOI:10.1016/j.surfcoat.2008.01.020

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Tensile properties of the alloys at the temperature range of 750–850 °C are illustrated in Fig. 2. It is evident from the results that the strength of both alloys drops with the increasing temperature. At 750 °C, the ultimate tensile strength (UTS) and yield strength (YS) of T486-Y2O3 alloy are 728 MPa and 588 MPa, which increase by about 60 MPa and 50 MPa compared with the TiAl alloy matrix respectively. Increasing the temperature to 850 °C, the increments of the UTS and YS are both below 10 MPa because of the weakened strengthening effect. Furthermore, the elongations of the investigated alloys are lower than 5% when the temperature is below 825 °C, whereas those are above 10% at 850 °C. A significant increase in the strain to fracture occurs when the test temperature increases to 850 °C. The results reveal that the BDTTs of both alloys are between 825 °C and 850 °C by tensile tests at the strain rate of 5 × 10−4 s−1. And Y2O3 addition has no effect on the brittle-ductile transition of T486 alloy. A more detail discussion can be found in Section 4.1.

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 434 MPa for AA2524?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The variations of mechanical properties before and after creep aging process with pre-strains between 0% and 6% are shown in Fig. 5. It can be clearly seen from Fig. 5 that the pre-strain increases the yield and tensile strengths of the studied alloy after creep aging process. When creep aging is initiated, an interesting observation can be found. For all pre-strained samples, the yield strength showed a drop after creep aging at 180 °C for 1 h, and then the yield strength increases at a high rate. The explanation for this result is that the cold-worked structure undergoes dislocation recovery , since there is no obvious reduce in yield strength for the un-strained sample. During the later stages of creep aging, the yield strength of AA2524 alloy reaches a peak stress, and then it drops, which is described by classic precipitation strengthening behavior . It can be observed from Fig. 5(a) that the pre-strain can significantly increase the peak yield strength and reduce the time to reach peak yield strength. The un-stretched sample can reach the peak yield strength of 341 MPa at about 12 h. But, for the samples with the pre-strains of 1.5%, 4% and 6%, the peak yield strength values are about 434 MPa, 468 MPa and 477 MPa, and the time required to achieve the peak yield strength are about 9 h, 9 h and 6 h, respectively. The tensile strength value reached in the test will be related to the dislocation density. The evolution of tensile strength for creep aged AA2524 under different pre-strains is shown in Fig. 5(b). Obviously, the tendency of tensile strength curve is similar to that of yield strength. However, the curve of 6% pre-strained samples show a longer reduction time in tensile strength during the initial creep stage. This indicates that the density of dislocation is decreased in the initial stage of the creep aging process.
DOI:10.1016/j.jallcom.2016.08.291

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 525 ± 36 MPa for NiCoCr?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):CrMnFeCoNi high entropy alloys holding several principle elements have displayed exceptional strength, ductility and damage tolerance, and these properties are even better at cryogenic temperature. Among CrMnFeCoNi and its derivatives, NiCoCr, called medium entropy alloy (MEA), has shown the best mechanical properties. Yet, research on the welding of NiCoCr is still in its infancy, which, to certain extent, renders the application of such advanced alloys in engineering structures. Here, for the first time, laser welding is employed to join NiCoCr MEA to achieve butt-type joints; microstructure and mechanical properties at 300 K and 77 K are then examined by experiments and molecular dynamic (MD) simulation. It is found that the ultimate tensile strength (UTS) and ductility of the weld at 300 K are 525 ± 36 MPa and 85 ± 15%, respectively. At 77 K, the UTS is enhanced to 828 ± 99 MPa but the ductility is reduced by 10%. The combination of UTS and ductility at both tested temperatures is the best among the joints of CrMnFeCoNi and its derivatives. The reason for this unprecedented combination is explored by EBSD and atomistic simulation. Slip bands and low-density deformation twins are found in the deformed microstructure of the weld fractured at 300 K as evidenced from EBSD observation. High-density twins besides the slip bands are seen in the weld deformed at 77 K. Real-time atomistic deformation is revealed from MD simulation, including dislocation nucleation and its motion, the formation of twin boundaries, and the interaction between the dislocation and twin boundaries. The unprecedented combination of strength and ductility in laser welded NiCoCr joints provides significant promise of NiCoCr MEA for application in engineering structures, especially at cryogenic circumstances.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2020.140501

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):To obtain high strength for near-rapid solidification (NRS) magnesium alloys, the microstructures and mechanical properties of the Mg94Zn2.4Y3.6 (at%) alloys, which were prepared by ordinary and injection copper mold casting (ICMC), respectively, were investigated comprehensively. It has been shown that the microstructure of ICMC Mg94Zn2.4Y3.6 alloy possesses the far-away-from equilibrium feature of NRS materials, which is composed of refined α-Mg grains with supersaturated Y and Zn, fine network-like long-period stacking-order (LPSO) phases and a few of Mg24Y5 particles. The interface between LPSO phase and α-Mg matrix is semi-coherent with the orientation relationship of [0002]α//[112 ̄0]LPSO, (101 ̄0)α//(0002)LPSO. The ICMC alloy exhibits enhanced mechanical properties with the ultimate tensile strength and elongation up to 355 MPa and 7% at room temperature, respectively, and with the ultimate tensile strength of 302 MPa at 150 °C. It can be concluded that the strengthening mechanisms of this ICMC alloy are attributed to the grain refinement, the solid solution effect, the secondary phase strengthening and the crystalline boundaries strengthening.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2016.10.071

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Major AM processes for HEAs prealloyed powder include directed energy deposition (DED), selective laser melting (SLM) and electron beam melting (EBM); due to higher cooling rate of SLM, finer grain structure and higher internal stress within HEAs processed by SLM have resulted higher tensile yield strength (YS) and ultimate tensile strength (UTS) than those processed by DED and EBM; a recent review on HEAs fabricated by SLM has reported that general YS values are about 402−773 MPa and UTS are about 480−1178 MPa []. Since SLM process has the potential to induce higher strength for HEAs than DED and EBM, the present study has focused on SLM process. SLM involves the fusion of powders layer by layer [,], and many engineering alloys have been studied with this process extensively []. For aged and hardened type alloys such as Inconel718, post heat treatment design would be required to eliminate detrimental Laves phase and promote γ′′ precipitation strengthening, in order to achieve tensile strengths comparable with those of conventional cast & wrought (C&W) materials [,], since SLM process can induce different phase transformation pathways due to the high internal stress and repeated laser scanning, Laves phase formation could occur in as-built condition and affect post ageing efficiencies for Inconel718 [].

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In this work, ellipsometry, Brillouin spectroscopy and nanoindentation are combined to assess the mechanical properties of a nanocrystalline Al2O3/a-Al2O3 composite coating with high accuracy and precision. The nanocomposite is grown by pulsed laser deposition at either room temperature or 600 °C. The adhesive strength is evaluated by nanoscratch tests. In the room temperature process the coating attains an unusual combination of compactness, strong interfacial bonding, moderate stiffness (E = 195 ± 9 GPa and ν = 0.29 ± 0.02) and significant hardness (H = 10 ± 1 GPa), resulting in superior plastic behavior and a relatively high ratio of hardness to elastic modulus (H/E = 0.049). These features are correlated to the nanostructure of the coating, which comprises a regular dispersion of ultrafine crystalline Al2O3 nanodomains (2–5 nm) in a dense and amorphous alumina matrix, as revealed by transmission electron microscopy. For the coating grown at 600 °C, strong adhesion is also observed, with an increase of stiffness and a significant enhancement of hardness (E = 277 ± 9 GPa, ν = 0.27 ± 0.02 and H = 25 ± 1 GPa), suggesting an outstanding resistance to wear (H/E = 0.091).
DOI:10.1016/j.actamat.2013.01.050

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The compressive true stress-true strain curves at high temperatures are plotted in the Supplementary Fig. 10 and the yield strength as a function of temperature is summarized in Fig. 5b. Noticeably, the Co0.80Cr1.40Fe0.82Ni0.16Hf0.30 eutectics exhibits a great high-temperature softening resistance, which is even better than Inconel 718 and most reported refractory high-entropy alloys (RHEA). This is probably related to its unique microstructure presenting two different eutectic morphologies (Fig. 3b and Supplementary Fig. 4d), which deserves further investigation in the near future. Interestingly, the Co0.81Cr0.16Fe0.83Ni1.43Hf0.35 eutectics has a larger yield strength at 600 °C than at the room temperature (695 vs 374 MPa, as listed in Supplementary Table 5), probably due to precipitation at high temperature. From the abovementioned experimental findings, it can be concluded that the eutectic MPEAs, predominantly consisting of soft solid solution (like FCC) and hard phase (like Laves), exhibit tunable mechanical performance due to the variation in eutectic compositions and microstructures, rendering them promising candidates to offer good room-temperature mechanical performance and excellent high-temperature strength.
DOI:10.1016/j.matdes.2022.110795

问题:Which compound has a yield strength of 280 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The tensile tests of the ZK60 and ZK60-Ca alloys in the solutionized, peak-aged and over-aged state have been conducted. The typical uniaxial tensile engineering stress-strain curves are shown in Fig. 1b. The corresponding mechanical properties of the two alloys under the different heat treatment conditions are summarized in Fig. 1c. In the soultionized state, the ZK60 alloy has tensile yield strength (TYS) of 100 MPa, ultimate tensile strength (UTS) of 280 MPa and elongation of 21.8%. In comparison, the soultionized ZK60-Ca alloy achieves higher TYS (106 MPa), UTS (295 MPa) and elongation (23.2%). In the peak-aged state, the strength of the alloys is significantly improved. The TYS and UTS of the peak-aged ZK60 and ZK60-Ca alloys are higher than that of the solutionized ZK60 and ZK60-Ca alloys, but the elongation is lower. The TYS and UTS of the peak-aged ZK60-Ca alloy is 184 MPa and 308 MPa, respectively, which are 17 MPa and 12 MPa higher than those of the peak-aged ZK60 counterpart. However, the elongation (15.5%) of the ZK60-Ca alloy is smaller than that of the ZK60 alloy (17.4%). In the over-aged state, the TYS, UTS and elongation decrease compared with the values in the peak-aged state. The TYS and UTS of the over-aged ZK60-Ca alloy are higher than those of the over-aged ZK60 alloy, but the elongation is a little smaller. The above results indicate that the mechanical behavior depends on the aging time and microalloying Ca addition, which has a large impact on the microstructure. Here we characterized the microstructures of the two alloys in details.
DOI:10.1016/j.jma.2022.06.011

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of 0Al?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In general, the DSA starts at the critical strain, where the interaction time of C-Mn couple with SFs is higher than the C-Mn reorientation time . At the high tensile strain rate, the interaction time between C-Mn couples and SFs is reduced. Besides, the Al in TWIP steel enhances the SFE and the activation energy for the re-orientation of the C atom, so the interaction time of C-Mn couple decreases and the orientation time of C atom raises. Therefore both the plastic deformation at high strain rate and the increase of Al element should suppress the DSA effect . The previous studies pointed out that the contribution of the DSA effect on flow stress was less than 80 MPa . However, the strength increment from DSA effect cannot explain the loss of flow strength with increasing strain rate in 0Al and 1.5Al TWIP steels. Moreover, the strength contribution from dislocation should enhance with increasing tensile strain rate, for the suppressed dislocation dynamic recovery during high strain rate deformation . Hence, the drop of tensile strength at high strain rate should derive from the DTB as discussed in Section 3.3.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2017.02.014

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The mechanical properties of SPEs are very important for the safety of batteries. Fig. 2c shows stress-strain curves of BC-g-PEGM-GPEs and its control sample (i.e. BC/PEGM mixture-based GPEs, denoted as BC/PEGM-GPEs). For BC/PEGM-GPEs, the tensile strength, Young's modulus, and toughness are as low as 0.2 MPa, 1.9 MPa, and 15.1 KJ/m3, respectively. In sharp constrast, the tensile strength and Young's modulus of BC-g-PEGM-GPEs are up to 2.0 MPa and 5.0 MPa, respectively. More importantly, the toughness of BC-g-PEGM-GPEs (316.7 KJ/m3) is 21 times as high as that of BC/PEGM-GPEs. Table S1 shows that when comparing the mechanical properties of BC-g-PEGM-GPEs to other reported SPEs, BC-g-PEGM-GPEs exhibit impressive tensile strength and Young's modulus. The results indicate BC-g-PEGM bottlebrushes provide superior mechanical properties to GPEs as compared to the conventional filler-containing GPEs (e.g. BC/PEGM-GPEs). This could be because the grafted PEGM side chains of BC-g-PEGM contain numerous homogeneously distributed diethylene glycol methyl ether groups that have a good affinity to the poly(ethylene glycol)-containing gel electrolyte. As a result, BC-g-PEGM bottlebrushes present a good interfacial compatibility with gel electrolytes and thus demonstrate superior mechanical properties.
DOI:10.1016/j.mtnano.2021.100128

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 550 MPa for W12?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):A direct comparison of the change in macroscopic lattice strain at 3 mm below the weld surface determined from the condition before and after loading to maximum load for the three LTT alloys investigated is shown in Fig. 12. As discussed before, the transition zone between IHAZ and OHAZ ranging from 6 mm to 8 mm from the weld centre line undergoes a substantial shift towards compression as a result of plastic deformation within this region as the local yield strength is exceeded by the external stress. The base metal remains more or less unaffected because the maximum load of 36 kN (33 kN in case of W12), which is equal to 600 MPa (550 MPa) within the base metal cross section, is approx. 87% (approx. 80%) of the base metal yield strength and as such purely elastic behaviour occurred. In opposite to the comparable lattice strain behaviour occurring in the IHAZ, the OHAZ and the base metal in all three test welds, the changes in macroscopic lattice strain within the FZ vary for each alloy tested. In test weld W8 (8 wt% Ni, 10 wt% Cr) a shift by approx. 180 μɛ from tension into compression occurred after loading. As the amount of retained austenite is negligible, this may be attributed to plastic deformation of the martensitic FZ material. As shown in Fig. 9 the external loading of test weld W10 (10 wt% Ni, 10 wt% Cr) resulted in substantially increased compression in the γ-phase while at the same time the phase fraction of retained austenite reduced. The lattice strain in the martensitic α′-phase remained constant, but the width of the FZ lattice strain plateau reduced slightly. Overall the reduced impact of the increased compressive lattice strain of the γ-phase due to the reduction in phase fraction resulted in a negligible reduction in macroscopic lattice strain in the FZ in W10.

问题:Which compound has a ductility of 0.8%?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Polycrystalline NiAl with the B2 structure exhibits no tensile ductility at room temperature due to the lack of five independent slip systems to satisfy the von Mises’ criterion for polycrystalline plasticity . Like many BCC metals and B2 aluminides, NiAl exhibits cleavage fracture. However, the cleavage plane is not well defined . It has been observed that in B2-NiAl, fracture of {511} transient planes may proceed before cleavage on {110} planes . Darolia et al. reported that small additions of Fe, Ga, and Mo significantly improve the tensile ductility, but Fe is the most effective. For example, NiAl along <110> has a tensile ductility of only 0.8%, but with the addition of 0.25 at.% Fe (substituting for Al in NiAl), the tensile ductility increases to 5.9% . In another study, Field et al. reported that the addition of Cr promotes the activation of the <111> slip system. Law and Rachinger et al. suggested that Cr, Mn, and Fe lower the ordering energy of NiAl and favor <100> slip, but this argument was disputed by Cotton . Small additions of Mo were also reported to increase the ductility of polycrystalline NiAl at room temperature , while Hahn and Vedula demonstrated that small deviations from the stoichiometric composition make NiAl even more brittle at low temperatures.
DOI:10.1016/j.intermet.2010.03.026

问题:Which compound has a yield strength of ∼283 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Al-based composites reinforced with TiB2 ceramics (1 wt.%, 2 wt.% and 5 wt.%) have been manufactured through selective laser melting (SLM). The results show that the densification, microstructure and mechanical properties of the SLM-processed composites are sensitive to the ceramic particle fraction. The average grain size of the composites decreased from ∼6.32 to ∼1.55 μm with increasing TiB2 fraction from 1 to 5 wt.% respectively. Fine equiaxed grains with narrow size distribution were obtained for composites with relatively high amount of TiB2 particles. The effects of TiB2 ceramic fraction on grain refinement and strengthening mechanisms of SLM-processed composites were discussed. Al-based composite with 2 wt.% TiB2 ceramic was determined to be optimum, especially in the view of manufacturing quality, grain refinement and mechanical properties. The composites at an optimum fraction of 2 wt.% TiB2 exhibited high microhardness of ∼127 HV0.2, tensile strength of ∼444 MPa, yield strength of ∼283 MPa and elongation of ∼4.2% owing to collective effects of dispersion and grain refinement strengthening as well as high degree of densification. This study can readily offer reference values for laser additive manufacturing of other metal matrix composites to obtain a good compromise of strength and ductility by tuning reinforcement fraction.
DOI:10.1016/j.jmrt.2020.04.059

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of FR-1?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):For FR-1 alloy (see Fig. 18a and Table 2), when it is heat-treated at the temperatures between 600 °C and 750 °C, the heat-treated alloy's ductility has been significantly improved compared to those of as-forged stage. Specifically, the ductility is continually improved with increasing the heat treatment temperature, showing a value of 4.0% for the 600 °C-treated one and 9.0% for the 750 °C-treated one. Showing the reverse tendency, the corresponding strength/hardness of the heat-treated FR-1 alloy is slightly and gradually reduced comparing to those of as-forged stage, having the UTS of 1420.2 MPa and hardness of 488.6 HV for the 600 °C-treated one, and the UTS of 1252.3 MPa and hardness of 410.2 HV for the 750 °C-treated one. Particularly, two excellent strength-ductility combinations are successfully achieved for FR-1 alloy heat-treated at 700 °C and 750 °C, with values of 1386.5 MPa/7.3% and 1252.3 MPa/9.0%, respectively. These strength-ductility balances are not only acceptable and qualified for metastable β titanium alloys in engineering applications but also be comparable to or even better than those of the metastable β titanium alloys (including Ti-5553 alloys) manufactured by other PM methods [] and IM counterparts [,,], as demonstrated in Fig. 19. Nevertheless, both the ductility and strength/hardness are lowered severely for the 800 °C and 850 °C heat-treated FR-1 alloy than those of as-forged stage, with the UTS lower than 1200 MPa, hardness lower than 400 HV, and εf lower than 4.1%.
DOI:10.1016/j.matdes.2021.109457

问题:What is the youngs modulus of stainless steel X2CrNi18-9?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The hardness evolution has been evaluated for both methods experimental and numerical one for different loads (Fig. 8). Fig. 8a. illustrates the obtained results. An increasing of the hardness can be observed when increasing the normal applied load. The hardness ranges are comprised between HBW = 115 to HBW = 157 and HBW = 133 to HBW = 184 for the numerical model and the experimental measurement, respectively. The obtained results are comparable to the hardness values published in the literature for the stainless steel X2CrNi18-9 . The young's modulus evolution is presented in Fig. 8b, showings higher value that in the simulation considered E-modulus at the lowest applied load F = 10 N (E = 177 GPa) and a lower one at F = 613 N (E = 147 GPa). The load dependency of the elastic modulus is the result of the plastic deformation. This can be explained by the fact that the stiffness values are more accurate at the lowest normal loads with less effect of the plastic deformation. The average value of the experimental result is E ≈ 172 GPa which corresponds nearly to the young's modulus taken from the literature and adopted in the numerical model. As conclusion, the numerical model provides good prediction of the hardness and the young's modulus when comparing to both experimental and literature findings, which confirms the reliability of the model.
DOI:10.1016/j.ijmecsci.2019.105142

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The mechanical properties in term of ultimate tensile strength (UTS), yield strength (YS), tensile strain (El), fracture toughness (KΙC) and Vicker's hardness (Hv) of the TiC-γ′/Ni composites after different thermal treatment condition are compared in Table 3. In this work, the S-samples exhibit superior mechanical properties than the A-samples. The following thermal treatment procedures certainly optimize the microstructure and contribute to the enhancement of properties. As for the composite fabricated at 1400 °C (A0), in comparison, the composites after annealing exhibit superior mechanical properties. The yield strengths of samples after annealing at 750 °C for 20, 50 and 100 h were about 675 ± 45(A1), 730 ± 39(A2) and 736 ± 41 MPa (A3), respectively. Correspondingly, the ultimate tensile strength of samples tended to be 808 ± 11, 850 ± 16 and 864 ± 33 MPa, whilst the tensile elongation were kept at about 4.46 ± 0.32, 4.51 ± 0.29 and 4.39 ± 0.4%, illustrating the ductility is less sensitive to the annealing time. However, as for S-samples, the yield strengths of S0 samples can be reached to 1078 ± 59 MPa with about 7.72 ± 0.52% ductility. After solid solution treatment and subsequent annealing at 750 °C for 20, 50 and 100 h, the yield strengths were about 1230 ± 25, 1239 ± 15 and 1217 ± 29 MPa, respectively. The tensile elongation were correspondingly determined to be about 6.45 ± 0.6%, 6.27 ± 0.48% and 5.84 ± 0.35%.

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The mechanical properties of the laminates of 40 vol.% BVF with different numbers of laminae are plotted in Fig. 5. As seen from Fig. 5 the mechanical properties of the composites increased with the increasing number of fibre laminae until the number reached 4. With further increase in the fibre laminae number to 5, the mechanical properties of the composites decreased. The tensile modulus and strength of the laminates with 40 vol.% BVF increased by 62% and 98%, respectively, as the fibre laminae number was increased to 4. The increased tensile properties indicated an increase in λ, which could be attributed to an increase in Vfb, as a linear pattern was observed: b(BVF40-2) = 0.14*2/aλ0, b(BVF40-3) = 0.14*3/aλ0, and b(BVF40-4) = 0.14*4/aλ0. Therefore, b∝nVf, where n is the number of fibre laminae. This finding corelated well with the result reportedby Prambauer et al. (2016). However, the tensile strength decreased to 39.75 MPa as the number of fibre laminae increased to five, while the tensile modulus of BF40-5 was not significantly different from that of BVF40-4. This implied the existence of a certain saturation point. Similar trends were also observed for the flexural and impact properties. The flexural modulus, flexural strength and impact strength of BVF40-4 were 140%, 80% and 122% higher than those of BF40-1, respectively. Whereas, the flexural modulus, flexural strength and impact strength of BF40-5 were reduced by 9%, 21% and 21% as the number of fibre mats were increased from 4 to 5.
DOI:10.1016/j.indcrop.2018.05.025

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 1.17 GPa for CoCrFeNiAl0.2?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In this study, CoCrFeNiAl0.2(TiC)x (x = 0.06, 0.12, 0.2) composites were successfully fabricated via the HGCS route, which uses cheap metal oxides and Al, Ti, C powder as raw materials. And the microstructure and mechanical properties of these composites were regulated by changing the ratio of raw materials and the value of the high-gravity field. With increasing the content of Ti, C and enlarging the value of the high-gravity field, the size of TiC precipitates increases from submicron to micron, and the grain refinement of the alloy matrix decreases, resulting in the decrease of the mechanical properties of the composites. In the research scope, when the high-gravity field raise to 1200 g, the optimal performance of bending strength, yield strength and specific strength of CoCrFeNiAl0.2(TiC)0.12 is up to 1.17 GPa, 2.13 GPa and 0.3 MPa·Kg·1m−3, respectively. The preparation process of these composites by HGCS is that the aluminothermic reaction first reacts and then Ti reacts with C to form TiC followed by the dissolution and precipitation principle. The in-situ synthesized TiC and the small amount of BCC phase are accompanied by enhancing the compressive strength, bending strength and hardness of composites by refining the grain of HEA. Therefore, the HGCS with “green” and high-efficiency properties is another potential way to prepare high-performance ceramic/HEAs composites.
DOI:10.1016/j.jallcom.2021.163221

问题:What is the ductility of CoCrNi2(Al0.2Nb0.2)?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Metallic alloys with high strength and large ductility are required for extreme structural applications. However, the achievement of ultrahigh strength often results in a substantially decreased ductility. Here, we report a strategy to achieve the strength-ductility synergy by tailoring the alloy composition to control the local stacking fault energy (SFE) of the face-centered-cubic (fcc) matrix in an L12-strengthened superlattice alloy. As a proof of concept, based on the thermodynamic calculations, we developed a non-equiatomic CoCrNi2(Al0.2Nb0.2) alloy using phase separation to create a near-equiatomic low SFE disordered CoCrNi medium-entropy alloy matrix with in situ formed high-content coherent Ni3(Al, Nb)-type ordered nanoprecipitates (∼ 12 nm). The alloy achieves a high tensile strength up to 1.6 GPa and a uniform ductility of 33%. The low SFE of the fcc matrix promotes the formation of nanotwins and parallel microbands during plastic deformation which could remarkably enhance the strain hardening capacity. This work provides a strategy for developing ultrahigh-strength alloys with large uniform ductility.
DOI:10.1016/j.fmre.2022.05.024

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of Cu40Zn?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The effect of titanium addition on the microstructure and mechanical properties of brass Cu40Zn has been studied via the powder metallurgy (P/M) route. The water-atomized Cu40Zn–1.0 wt% Ti alloy powder was consolidated at different temperatures in the range of 400–600 °C using spark plasma sintering (SPS) and hot extrusion subsequently. Results show that the super-saturated solid solution titanium element in rapidly cooled brass Cu40Zn powder created high chemical potential for a precipitate reaction, showing significant grain refinement effects on the consolidated Cu40Zn matrix. Consequently, excellent mechanical properties were obtained by precipitation hardening and work hardening after sintering and extrusion, with yield strength of 390 MPa, ultimate tensile strength of 617 MPa, and Vickers micro-hardness of 192 HV, which are 28.7%, 23.4%, and 23.9% higher values than those of extruded Cu40Zn brass, respectively.
DOI:10.1016/j.jmst.2013.08.013

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 248 MPa for AXM070305?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):A dilute Mg-0.68Al-0.32Ca-0.50Mn (AXM070305, wt%) magnesium (Mg) alloy with high strength and excellent ductility was developed by extrusion and subsequent T6 heat treatment. The alloy exhibits a yield strength (YS) of 248 MPa, ultimate tensile strength (UTS) of 288 MPa and elongation to failure (EL) of 21.0%, after T6 peak-aged treatment. The high strength is mainly attributed to the presence of high number density of nanosized β-Mn precipitates, a large number of mono-layered Guinier-Preston (G.P.) zones and planar Al2Ca phase. The excellent ductility is mainly due to the fully recrystallized fine grains with weak basal texture. The peak microhardness of this alloy was reached 67.9 HV after aging at 200 °C for 16 h, due to the precipitation of mono-layered G.P. zone, nanosized particle β-Mn phase and planar Al2Ca phase.

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):It displays in Table 2 and Fig. 8 that both as-printed and heat-treated conditions present consistent mechanical properties along different directions (i.e., BD and TD). The tensile strength and yield strength along the TD in the as-printed condition are 1745 ± 27 and 1057 ± 15 MPa, respectively. These strength values increase to 1960 ± 41 and 1380 ± 38 MPa, respectively, after heat treatment. It is well understood that the increase in strength is attributed to the carbide precipitation and elimination of retained austenite during heat treatment. Accordingly, the elongation along the TD reduces from 22.0 ± 3.0% to 16.2 ± 2% during heat treatment. However, it is interesting to note that the toughness along the TD increases from 11 ± 1 J in the as-printed condition to 35 ± 5 J in the heat-treated condition. This observation is due to the formation of tempered martensite in the heat-treated condition that improves the toughness. When comparing with the wrought 420SS in the same heat-treated condition (see Table 2), it depicts that the LPBF 420SS has larger tensile and yield strength, higher elongation, and superior toughness than the wrought material, demonstrating the feasibility of 420SS material fabricated by the current LPBF process.

Before the appearance of deformation mechanism transformation, the fatigue strength becomes increasing as the UTS improves; and as the deformation mechanism changes into the localized SBs deformation, the fatigue strength reduces with further strengthening (see Fig. 4(b) and (d)). Furthermore, according to the experimental results , it can be calculated through the parabolic relation that the optimal fatigue strength of Cu-5Al alloy can be obtained at the point that the UTS is about 540 MPa (see Fig. 4(b)). In contrast, according to the assumption proposed above, the optimal fatigue strength of Cu-5Al alloy may be gained at the intersection point in Fig. 4(a), and the engineering UTS for the intersection point is about 496 MPa. 问题:Which compound has a ultimate tensile strength of 161 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The Al-Ce system exhibits a eutectic at 10 wt.% (2.1 at.%) Ce [,], with an as-cast microstructure consisting of submicron Al11Ce3 platelets with “Chinese script” morphology dispersed in a nearly pure Al matrix. At ambient temperature, near-eutectic Al-Ce alloys exhibit modest ultimate tensile strength (UTS = 161 MPa for cast Al-12 wt.% Ce alloy) but, unlike Al-Ni eutectic alloys, they have high tensile ductility (13.5% for cast Al-12 wt.% Ce) [,]. Additional alloying elements such as Mg, Cu, and Fe can provide further solid-solution and precipitation strengthening [,,]. The mechanical properties of these Al-Ce-based alloys are promising at both ambient and elevated temperatures. The tensile strength of cast Al-10Mg-8Ce (wt.%) is 228 MPa with 1% elongation at ambient temperature. Its UTS decreases to 137 and 97 MPa at 260 and 315 °C, respectively, while elongation increases to 4 and 20 %, respectively []. The elevated temperature strength of Al-10Mg-8Ce is much higher than that of common aluminum piston alloys used at elevated temperatures, such as 2618-T61 (Al-2.3Cu-1.6Mg-1.1Fe-1.1Ni, wt.%), which has UTS of 90 and 52 MPa at 260 and 315 °C, respectively []. The strength of Al-10Mg-8Ce is comparable to that of the commercial alloy A319-T7 (Al-8.6Si-3.8Cu-0.36Mg-0.5Fe-0.3Mn, wt.%) used in engine blocks, whose ambient temperature UTS decreases from 295 to 162 MPa at 275 °C []. In addition, Al-Ce-based eutectic alloys can be extruded [], as well as processed via powder-based and direct-write additive manufacturing [], while maintaining excellent mechanical properties. Extrusion of Al-10Mg-8Ce with a 5:1 extrusion ratio increases the ambient-temperature tensile strength to 375 MPa (64% increase) and tensile elongation to 5.8%. An as-printed Al-3Ce-7Cu (wt.%) alloy produced by selective laser melting has a UTS of 160–176 MPa and tensile elongation of 11–18 % at 250 °C [].
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2019.138440

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 1456 MPa for Ti2Ni?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The engineering stress–strain curves obtained under uniaxial compression are presented in Fig. 2 and the data are summarized in Table 1. The fracture strength is 1456 MPa with a high Young's modulus of about 155 GPa and 1% elastic strain. On the other hand, alloy B exhibits an obvious elastic–plastic transition and pronounced ductility under compressive loading (Fig. 2). Yielding occurs at 1313 MPa and the alloy fails at a stress of about 1989 MPa and a strain of 2.9%, respectively. The Young's modulus of this alloy is 128 GPa. Compared to alloy A, alloy B is softer (lower yield strength and lower Young's modulus) and reveals distinct plasticity (Fig. 2), indicating an enhancement in ductility by increasing the volume fraction of the dendritic β-Ti(Ta) solid solution in the material. Although the Young's modulus of alloy B is lower than that of alloy A, it is still much higher than the values of most other Ti-base DNUS alloys (60–100 GPa) and Ti-base BMGs (93–102 GPa) and even conventional crystalline Ti alloys, such as Ti–6Al–4V (∼120 GPa) . In the case of alloy A, the presence of the ultrafine-structured matrix and the Ti2Ni intermetallics in the microstructure are believed to contribute to the high yield strength and the high Young's modulus. Since not enough micrometer-sized ductile β-phase dendrites exist in the microstructure, alloy A exhibits almost zero plasticity. Obviously, the presence of Ti2Ni in the microstructure aggravates brittleness.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2006.02.236

问题:Which compound has a yield strength of ~60 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):From Fig. 6b, we note that the σy of BJP specimens is lower (by ~60 MPa or ~20%) than that of 316L SS with similar grain size predicted by the Hall-Petch relation. This reduction is possibly due to the relatively high porosity levels in these specimens, as pores can raise the stress locally and result in the onset of plastic deformation at lower stress levels [,]. Mil'man , Bocchini , and Klar et. al. reported that σy of sintered ferrous alloys decreases with the porosity level according to the equation proposed by Ryshkevitch : σy = σy,0 × exp(-6.5f), where σy,0 is the yield strength of fully dense alloy (taken as 260 MPa for 316L with d ~ 26 μm ) and f is the pore volume fraction. For the range of f observed in the BJP alloys examined in the present work (3.7 to 5.6%), the above relation predicts σy between 181 and 204 MPa, which is in good agreement with the experimentally measured range of 189 and 198 MPa (see Table 2).
DOI:10.1016/j.actamat.2020.08.033

They reported the maximum shear strength of ~360.9 MPa for the bonding processed at 1248 K (975 °C) using 20 MPa for 90 minutes. Ghosh et al. prepared the diffusion-bonded Ti-5.5Al-2.4V and 304 SS in the temperature range of 1123 K to 1223 K (850 °C to 950 °C) using 3 MPa uniaxial compressive load for 1 hour in vacuum. 问题:Which compound has a ultimate tensile strength of 276 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In this work, solution and low-temperature (50–125 °C) aging treatment is utilized to develop a novel Mg-Li alloy with an excellent combination of strength and ductility. After optimizing the solution time, effects of aging treatment on microstructure and mechanical properties of the Mg-8Li-3Al-2Zn-0.5Y alloy were investigated. The highest strength is obtained after solution heat treatment at 350 °C for 4 h, accompanied with relatively low ductility. The following low-temperature aging treatment is used to balance the strength and ductility. With the increase of aging temperature, the ductility of the alloy was improved, accompanied by continued decrease in strength. The formation and aggregation of Al-Li phase during aging treatment is the major factor that influences the mechanical properties of the aged alloys. The reduced solid solution strengthening of Al and the aggregation of Al-Li phase are detrimental to strength. The evolution of Al-Li phase during aging treatment at different temperature is analyzed in detail. Through promoting the precipitation and preventing the aggregation of Al-Li phase by optimizing aging temperature, well-designed low-temperature aging treatment (75 °C × 4 h) results in favorable microstructural evolution and optimum combination of strength (yield strength = 216 MPa, ultimate tensile strength = 276 MPa) and ductility (elongation = 11.1%).
DOI:10.1016/j.jallcom.2019.03.350

问题:What is the yield strength of 304L?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The shot-peened 304L steel is a composite-like material, with differences between the austenite and martensite grains. The created martensite grains play the role of hard phase embedded in the soft austenite phase. Therefore, high-level internal stresses are expected to be induced in the martensite phase. Before the shot peening process, the yield strength of austenite was around 200 MPa. Since it was not possible to obtain a fully martensitic sample using phase transformation, the yield strength of martensite was not determined. However, in a previous work performed on similar material , it was shown that elastic stress of the order of 1 GPa could be generated in martensite. Such a high-level of stress in martensite was also observed by Altenberger et al. . Thus, for the values of plastic deformation used in these experiments, it is likely that the martensite phase behaves elastically.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2010.06.008

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 255 ± 8 MPa for GWZ1031K?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):A high-strength Mg-10Gd-3Y–1Zn-0.4Zr (GWZ1031K, wt%) alloy was prepared by laser powder bed fusion (LPBF), and the microstructure and mechanical properties of the as built, LPBF-T5, LPBF-T4, and LPBF-T6 states were systematically studied. The as built alloy is composed of fine equiaxed ɑ-Mg grains with an average grain size of 4.1 ± 0.5 μm, reticular β-(Mg,Zn)3(Gd,Y) eutectic phase and flaky Y2O3 oxide phase, and exhibits yield strength (YS) of 310 ± 8 MPa, ultimate tensile strength (UTS) of 347 ± 6 MPa and elongation (EL) of 4.1 ± 0.8%. A simple direct aging heat treatment at 175 °C for 64 h after LPBF leads to an ultra-high YS of 365 ± 12 MPa but a low EL of 0.8 ± 0.3% in the LPBF-T5 alloy. The solution heat treatment improves ductility by transforming the hard and brittle eutectic phase into the relatively soft and deformable lamellar long period stacking ordered (LPSO) structure inside grains and X phase at grain boundaries without obvious grain growth. Moreover, the LPBF-T4 alloy solution-treated at 450 °C for 12 h exhibits YS of 255 ± 8 MPa, UTS of 328 ± 9 MPa, and EL of 10.3 ± 0.5%. Aging heat treatment after solution introduces numerous prismatic β′ and β1 precipitates, which help to increase tensile strength. The YS, UTS, and EL of the LPBF-T6 alloy are 316 ± 5 MPa, 400 ± 7 MPa, and 2.2 ± 0.3%, respectively. It can be concluded that the LPBF process when combined with specially designed post-processing holds great promise for the manufacturing of high-performance components of the Mg-rare earth alloys with significantly higher YS for various applications.
DOI:10.1016/j.addma.2021.102517

问题:Which compound has a yield strength of ∼1061 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Different from the common strategy to tailor elastic modulus by controlling β phase, an ultralow elastic modulus of 42 GPa and a high yield strength of ∼1061 MPa were obtained in the rolled TiZr by producing nanoscale α′′ martensitic lamellas with a volume fraction of ∼14%. The ultralow elastic modulus can be mainly attributed to the inherent low elastic modulus of α′′ martensite []. At the same time, the pre-martensitic lattice softening should also contribute to the low elastic modulus of the rolled TiZr due to the phase transformation from α′′ phase to α′ phase during test deformation. A similar decrease in elastic modulus induced by lattice softening also has been observed in other metastable β titanium alloys at room temperature [,]. The high yield strength results from the strengthening of high-density α′′ phase boundaries. Both the low elastic modulus and high strength result in an ultrahigh EAS of 2.51%. Unfortunately, further increasing the strain from 0.69 to 2.30, α′′ phase disappear due to its transformation to α′ phase. The α′ phase normally exhibits a similar elastic modulus as β phase []. As a result, undesirable increases in both elastic modulus and yield strength happened. Surprisingly, the corresponding EAS values with high strains are still much higher than reported values. It may be attributed to the existence of high-density dislocations and α′ phase boundaries. Firstly, when the stress is applied, the bow out of dislocation lines locked by dislocation forest gives extra elastic deformation [,]. Secondly, the atoms at phase boundaries tend to deviate from the equilibrium position and exhibit looser structure than the interior of crystal, leading to weak bonding force between atoms []. These two factors lead to a low elastic modulus and contribute to the high EAS values.
DOI:10.1016/j.jallcom.2018.11.388

问题:Which compound has a ductility of 5%?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Implant designs may contain intended or unintended stress concentrators such as fillets, grooves, cracks, notches, bends or holes due to the manufacturing process, service applications or damages developed during service. When a ductile material such as CP-titanium is deformed in the presence of a stress concentrator, a phenomenon known as ‘‘notch strengthening’’ may occur . Due to this phenomenon, it is possible for the axial yield stress of the notched sample to be greater than that of the un-notched one. On the other hand, the occurrence of a ‘‘notch weakening’’ effect when the ductility of the un-notched-bar is less than 5% or 6% has been reported . Although the notch weakening effect is frequently considered in the design of engineering components, less attention has been paid to the notch strengthening phenomenon as a mechanism for the load bearing capacity improvement of engineering components. Therefore, it is of interest to analyze the behavior of CP-titanium as a biomaterial in the presence of design-related stress-risers.
DOI:10.1016/j.matdes.2013.09.077

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Thanks to the application needs of conformal cooling channels in mold field, selective laser melting (SLM) has become a key technique for fabricating novel mold with high cooling efficiency. This work systematically investigates the action mechanism of SLM processing parameters on densification of 18Ni300 maraging steel, and corresponding phase identification, microstructural evolution, nanoprecipitation behaviors and mechanical properties of as-built samples with optimal relative density (RD) before and after solution-aged treatment. Response surface method and analysis of variance are employed to analyze the significance levels of effects for SLM processing parameters on RD. The optimal processing parameter set and resultant RD of 99.45% as well as corresponding processing windows for SLMed 18Ni300 sample can be obtained. It is found that as-built sample consists of major martensite α phase and scarce austenite γ phase. Solution-aged treatment is in favor of reversion of martensite to austenite and precipitation of intermetallic compounds. Both as-built and solution-aged samples exhibit no obvious texture fiber, but there are different grain sizes and orientations. It is worthy noted that the mechanical properties (ultimate tensile strength of ∼1245 MPa) of SLMed 18Ni300 sample are comparable to that of corresponding wrought sample.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2021.142334

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 15% for AM60?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The mechanical properties of the Mg alloy AM60 can be improved significantly by severe plastic deformation (SPD). The lower the temperature (down to 150 °C) of equal channel angular pressing (ECAP) the higher is the resulting strength (up to 310 MPa) which can be ascribed to the concomitant decrease of grain size (down to 1 μm). After ECAP-processing at temperatures 150–210 °C the ductility remains at about the same high level (∼15%) as in the initial material. This is explained with the presence of Al12Mg17 precipitates with a size of about 500 nm, which decrease the remaining concentration of Al in the solid solution of the matrix. Differential scanning calorimetry (DSC) revealed four peaks during heating runs. The most remarkable peak occurs at 390 °C in the initial sample, and at 360 °C in the material ECAPed at 150 °C. Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) analyses showed that this peak can be associated with the dissolution of the Al12Mg17 precipitates as well as with the annealing of dislocations and possibly vacancy clusters, and that ECAP has the potential to induce a shift of a phase boundary to lower temperatures because of ECAP induced lattice defects.
DOI:10.1007/s10853-006-0977-8

问题:What is the yield strength of ZrCu?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Since last decade, considerable attention has been focused on the bulk metallic glasses (BMGs) due to their unique properties . A series of BMG-forming systems with critical diameters more than 20 mm have been developed. They are Zr- , Pd- , Y- , Pt- , Mg- , La- and ZrCu-based alloys. Among them, ZrCu-based alloys are considered to be a promising system because it exhibits (1) excellent glass forming ability (GFA) up to 25 mm BMG prepared by copper mould injection; (2) excellent mechanical properties: yield strength 1822 MPa, fracture strength 2163 MPa, Vicker’s hardness 533 ± 3; (3) large compressive plastic deformation up to 15%; (4) relative cheap raw materials (no expensive Pd, Pt and Sc elements); (5) non-toxic elements (e.g., Be); (6) good potential biocompatible property (no Ni element) . To have industrial applications of the novel ZrCu-base BMG, one has to address the life-time (corrosion behavior) of the BMG under various environmental conditions. In our preliminary corrosion study of the ZrCu-based BMGs, it was found that the BMG alloy has high corrosion resistance in H2SO4 solution, but poor corrosion resistance in 3% NaCl and 1 N HCl. Thus, it is of interest to improve corrosion resistance of the BMG alloy in chloride-containing solutions and understanding the pitting mechanism.
DOI:10.1016/j.jnoncrysol.2008.10.011

问题:What is the yield strength of AZ31 Mg?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Mechanical milling was used to prepare nanocrystalline AZ31 magnesium (Mg) alloy with titanium (Ti) addition. During milling, the crystallite size of the AZ31 Mg matrix decreased steadily with the increase of milling time. After milling for 110 h, the crystallite size of the Mg phase of the alloy with 18 wt.% and 27 wt.% Ti additions was refined to 86 nm and 66 nm respectively, leading to the formation of a nanocrystalline Mg matrix microstructure. Meanwhile, the size of Ti particles added in the alloy also decreased gradually with the increase of milling time, and the Ti dispersions presented a positive effect on the refinement of the Mg matrix grains. By milling for 110 h, the average size of Ti particles was refined to less than 1 μm in the AZ31 Mg alloy with 18 wt.% Ti addition. After mechanical milling, the as-milled powders were cold pressed into green compacts, and the mechanical properties were characterized by Vickers' hardness and uni-axial compressive tests. It was found that the micro-hardness of mechanically milled AZ31 Mg alloy with 27 wt.% Ti addition reached about 147 Hv, which was almost three times larger than that of the as-received AZ31 Mg alloy. Meanwhile, the yield strength of mechanically milled Ti-added AZ31 Mg alloy was seen to be as high as 293 MPa at room temperature and 60 MPa at 300 °C, respectively.
DOI:10.1016/j.matchar.2016.01.014

The base materials with the addition of over 2 wt% Mn showed a relatively good balance of strength and ductility when Si content was 2 wt% due to the transformation induced plasticity effect of retained austenite. 问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is ~60 MPa for 316L SS?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):From Fig. 6b, we note that the σy of BJP specimens is lower (by ~60 MPa or ~20%) than that of 316L SS with similar grain size predicted by the Hall-Petch relation. This reduction is possibly due to the relatively high porosity levels in these specimens, as pores can raise the stress locally and result in the onset of plastic deformation at lower stress levels [,]. Mil'man , Bocchini , and Klar et. al. reported that σy of sintered ferrous alloys decreases with the porosity level according to the equation proposed by Ryshkevitch : σy = σy,0 × exp(-6.5f), where σy,0 is the yield strength of fully dense alloy (taken as 260 MPa for 316L with d ~ 26 μm ) and f is the pore volume fraction. For the range of f observed in the BJP alloys examined in the present work (3.7 to 5.6%), the above relation predicts σy between 181 and 204 MPa, which is in good agreement with the experimentally measured range of 189 and 198 MPa (see Table 2).

问题:Which compound has a ductility of 19%?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The extensive hardening during HPT-deformation increases with the increasing HPT-processing temperature. The alloy with initial fully lamellar microstructure displays a yield and ultimate tensile strength, which are 640 MPa and 734 MPa, respectively, higher in comparison with the undeformed state. The microstructural changes produced through the HPT-deformation at 500 °C enhance the strength more effectively when the alloy initially has a martensitic microstructure. In this case, the HPT-processed alloy exhibits a yield and ultimate tensile strength as high as 1400 MPa and 1550 MPa, respectively. The HPT-deformation at 500 °C also contributes to further improvement in ductility of alloy with initial acicular microstructure. According to Sergueeva et al. , such unusual increase in ductility (elongation to failure is about 19%) can be associated with the appearance of the high amount of fine β phase particles, which inhibit significant grain growth and promote grain boundary sliding. The crystallographic texture of the predominant α phase developed in the HPT-deformed alloy may also contribute to the enhancing elongation to failure. A similar observation was recently reported for the Ti-6Al-4V alloy with an initial martensitic structure subjected to the warm symmetric and asymmetric rolling . As shown in this study, the inclination of the basal texture from the normal towards the rolling direction could contribute to an increase in the tensile elongation and a remarkable tensile strength-ductility balance.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2018.08.094

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 18.08 GPa for TiC?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The presence of vacancies at the interface has deep implication on many aspects, such as alloying segregation, pipe diffusion, and H trapping, etc. In the transition metal carbides and nitrides, vacancies existed in the crystal can also influence the bonding properties and its mechanical response []. Generally, it is expected that vacancies reduce the number of chemical bonds and hence lower the strength of materials. Thus, we also use the heterostructure with a vacancy in the interfacial region to suffer the tensile loading. Vacancy is only considered at the nonmetal C sublattice in the 1 atomic-layer TiC, because the abundant vacancies in the precipitations are detected to form the Ti4C3 based on experimental observations. Fig. 6 shows the plots of the stress-strain curve for the heterostructure with the vacancy effect during the tensile strength simulations. The ideal tensile strength obtained is 18.08 GPa with a vacancy at the interface, which is lower than that in heterostructure with 1 atomic-layer TiC, suggesting that the interfacial vacancy is detrimental to the interfacial strengthening. We anticipated that this softening effect resulted from interfacial vacancy also exist in the V-base precipitations, since theoretical literature shows that the bond in V4C3 is weaker than the bond V–C in bulk VC []. In addition to these influences, we found that the calculated lattice parameter of Ti4C3 with the rock-salt structure is 4.313 Å, which is slight lower that in bulk TiC, meaning that the interfacial vacancies be beneficial to release the interfacial strains to promote the growth and coarsening of the precipitations.
DOI:10.1016/j.jnucmat.2019.151821

问题:Which compound has a yield strength of 1450 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Austenitic stainless steels are widely used engineering materials in industries owing to their excellent corrosion and oxidation resistance, high work-hardening, and good formability . One of the major drawbacks of austenitic stainless steels is their low yield strength, usually being 150–300 MPa in the annealed states, which limits their technological applications. Strengthening austenitic stainless steels without compromising other useful properties is challenging. For example, strong austenitic stainless steels containing martensite or dispersed precipitates are usually less ductile and less corrosion resistant . Structural refinement is another option for strengthening, which can be induced by plastic deformation such as cold rolling (CR) , surface mechanical attribution treatment (SMAT) , equal channel angular pressing (ECAP) , and high pressure torsion (HPT) . Although structural refinement can result in a record-high strength, ductility and work-hardening are decreased considerably. Chen et al. produced a nanostructured 316L steel by using SMAT with a yield strength as high as 1450 MPa, but its work-hardening diminishes and elongation-to-failure drops to only ∼3%. In fact, the plastic straining induced ductility loss is more pronounced than the strength gain for the austenitic stainless steels, exhibiting a typical “banana-shaped” strength–ductility trade-off . In addition, martensite phases are frequently induced by plastic deformation in austenitic steels, which is detrimental to ductility and corrosion resistance.
DOI:10.1016/j.actamat.2011.11.009

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is ~1850 MPa for Fe42Mn28Co10Cr15Si5?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):This paper describes the main results from an experimental investigation into tailoring the phase content and grain structure for high strength of a microstructurally flexible high entropy alloy (HEA), Fe42Mn28Co10Cr15Si5 (in at%), using rolling, friction stir processing (FSP), and compression. Optical microscopy, neutron diffraction, and electron backscatter diffraction were employed to characterize microstructure and texture evolution. The material upon rolling was found to have triplex structure consisting of metastable gamma austenite (γ), stable sigma (σ), and stable epsilon martensite (ε) phases. The adaptive phase stability exhibited by the selected HEA of very low stacking fault energy with strain, strain rate, and temperature was used along with grain refinement to enhance the strength. To this end, the complex structure was refined by FSP at 350 revolutions per minute (RPM) tool rotation rate, while increasing the fraction of γ and decreasing the σ and ε content. The strength was further enhanced by FSP at 150 RPM by further refinement of the grain structure and increasing the fraction of ε phase. The peak ultimate tensile strength of ~1850 MPa was achieved by double pass FSP (350 RPM followed by 150 RPM), the sequence which even more refined the microstructure and increased the fraction of σ phase. While the content of diffusion created σ phase remains constant during subsequent compression, the fraction of ε increases due to the diffusionless strain induced γ→ε phase transformation. The transformation facilitates plastic strain accommodation and rapid strain hardening, which has been attributed to the increase in highly dislocated ε phase fraction and transformation induced dynamic Hall-Petch-type barrier effect. Interestingly, a great deal of hardening ability was exhibited by the HEA even at very high strength. Roles of texture, grain size, and phase content on the transformation during compression have been rationalized and discussed in this paper.
DOI:10.1016/j.jallcom.2021.161871

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 118 MPa for Mg-5Sn-3Sn-1Ca?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In this study, enhancement of mechanical properties of Mg-5Sn-3Zn alloy was achieved by alloying with Ca, and the strengthening response due to coarse CaMgSn, nano and micro-sized Mg2Sn precipitates in hot-extruded Mg-5Sn-3Zn based alloys was reported. As the content of Ca increases, the amount of Mg2Sn and CaMgSn precipitates in Mg-5Sn-3Zn based alloys increases while the average particle size of those precipitates decreases. Mg2Sn phases in both micrometer and nanometer scale are observed in Mg-5Sn-3Zn alloys with and without Ca. Besides micro-sized and nano-sized Mg2Sn, coarse precipitates of CaMgSn are observed in Mg-5Sn-3Zn with Ca addition. By alloying with Ca, grains are refined and fiber {0002} texture in hot-extruded Mg-5Sn-3Zn based alloys is weakened. The Mg-5Sn-3Sn-1Ca alloys show the largest YS of 118 MPa, UTS of 275 MPa and elongation of 21.9%. Improvement in combined strength and ductility of Mg-5Sn-3Sn-1Ca alloys is attributed to less coarse CaMgSn precipitates, more and finer nano-sized Mg2Sn precipitates, and a weak {0002} fiber texture.
DOI:10.1016/j.jallcom.2017.01.274

问题:What is the yield strength of Mo2C @ RGO/Cu?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In summary, the Mo2C@RGO/Cu composite with a uniform distribution of 1 vol% Mo2C@RGO and a well-preserved structural integrity of RGO was prepared by combined vertex-mixing/low-energy ball-milling and SPS. The grown Mo2C nanoparticles not only were firmly attached on RGO, but also formed a semi-coherent interface with Cu matrix, leading to strong interfacial bonding between the Mo2C@RGO and Cu matrix. The 1 vol% Mo2C@RGO/Cu composite exhibited a YS/UTS of 238/292 MPa, which were 58%/26% and 127%/57% higher than those of 1 vol% RGO/Cu composite and pure Cu, respectively. The significantly enhanced strength in Mo2C@RGO/Cu composite was attributed mainly to the Mo2C-induced robust interface. The RGO/Cu composite exhibited an oxygen-mediated interface that had a relatively weak interfacial bonding, causing moderate strength enhancement. The strengthening mechanism of Mo2C@RGO/Cu composite was proposed to be highly dependent on the dual role of Mo2C nanoparticles that not only enhanced the load transfer strengthening of RGO (∼85% proportion) but may also provide the Orowan strengthening themselves (∼15% proportion). Nonetheless, the introduced Mo2C nanoparticles resulted in an enhanced reduction in CTE but a decreased TC for Mo2C@RGO/Cu composite compared to pure Cu and RGO/Cu composite. These findings imply that the Mo2C@RGO is a promising reinforcement for the development of high-strength graphene/metal composites with low CTE, but it may not be suitable for optimizing the TC property of graphene/metal composites.
DOI:10.1016/j.compositesa.2018.03.014

问题:What is the yield strength of Mg-6Al-1Zn?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Prominent more stable mechanical properties are observed in as-annealed alloys compared to as-extruded alloys, as shown in Fig. 17. The stress-strain curve is highly coincident for as-annealed alloys of the same composition. It may be noted that mechanical properties of the alloys are greatly improved by annealing []. The main effect of residual stress on mechanical properties is that the existence of residual stress leads to local cracks in compression process, resulting in instability phenomenon. However, residual stress disappears after annealing. In addition, dynamic recrystallization of the alloys is not complete during extrusion process, and then accumulation of deformation energy storage can cause the alloys to be static recrystallization in annealing process []. In conclusion, mechanical properties of the alloys are more stable after annealing. UCS and 0.2% YS are 401 MPa and 203 MPa for Mg-6Al-1Zn alloy, respectively; when Fe concentration reaches to 1 wt%, UCS and 0.2% YS are 337 MPa and 149 MPa, respectively. UCS and 0.2% YS decrease sharply with the addition of Fe. However, 0.2% YS is stabilized at about 176–182 MPa, and compressive strength is about 356–366 MPa with increasing of Fe concentrations from 3 to 7 wt%. Mg-6Al-1Zn-3Fe has the highest UCS, 390 MPa before annealing, UCS of the alloys has the maximum of 360 MPa after annealing. Although UCS of the alloys before annealing is higher than that of annealed, its stability is very poor. Therefore, the alloys have better comprehensive mechanical properties after annealing. It is worth noting that, although 0.2% YS of the alloys after annealing is slightly lower than that of as-extruded alloys, the YS of the alloys still meets the strength requirements of fracturing balls (>70 MPa).
DOI:10.1016/j.jmst.2019.04.012

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Nb-doped CrMnFeCoNix (x = 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8 and 1) high entropy alloys were prepared by vacuum induction melting. The effects of Ni and Nb content on the microstructure evolution and tensile properties of the alloys were investigated. With increasing Ni content, the CrMnFeCoNix alloy changed its structure from a combination of face-centered cubic phase and σ phase to a single face-centered cubic phase. The yield strength, ultimate tensile strength and ductility of CrMnFeCoNix alloys first increased and then decreased with the increase of Ni content. Compared with other CrMnFeCoNix alloys, CrMnFeCoNi0.8 HEA has shown superior mechanical properties, exhibiting a high yield strength (240.9 MPa), ultimate tensile strength (527.5 MPa) and an impressive elongation-to-failure (62.5%). The addition of Nb significantly increased the volume fraction of Laves phase in the CrMnFeCoNi0.8 alloy, which further enhanced the tensile properties of CrMnFeCoNi0.8 high entropy alloys. The yield strength and ultimate tensile strength of the CrMnFeCoNi0.8Nb0.2 alloy were determined to be 406.9 MPa and 662.8 MPa, representing a 68.9% and 25.6% increase over CrMnFeCoNi0.8 alloy.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2021.140959

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 721 MPa for C18?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The tensile properties of the present alloy at 600 °C are shown in Fig. 3(b). The YS and the UTS for the uncoated (UC) alloy at the above temperature were 597 and 721 MPa, respectively. Similar to that observed at RT, a reduction in alloy strength (YS and UTS) was also noticed at 600 °C. The trend of progressive decrease in YS with increase in coating thickness was also observed at 600 °C, as evident from the YS values of 580, 567 and 523 MPa measured for C5, C18 and C35, respectively (Fig. 3(b)). A similar trend was also maintained in the UTS values of the coated alloy (Fig. 3(b)), with UTS of C35 at 590 MPa, being nearly 20% lower than that of UC. It is evident from Fig. 3(b) that the ductility value of 11.5% measured for UC at 600 °C was very similar to that measured at RT (Fig. 3(a)). However, the coated alloy showed a much higher ductility at 600 °C than at RT. The ductility of the coated alloy at 600 °C lied in the range 14.5–20.5%, which was significantly higher than the corresponding value of 11.5% for UC. Further, among the coated samples, a decrease in the ductility value from about 20% to 14.5% was observed as the coating thickness increased from 5 μm to 35 μm. As was done at RT, the coating-removed samples UC5, UC18 and UC35 samples were also tested at 600 °C and the tensile properties of these samples are shown in Fig. 3(b). As clearly evident, YS, UTS and ductility of UC5, UC18 and UC35 were very similar to the corresponding values of UC. 问题:Which compound has a yield strength of 428 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The compressive stress-strain curves of the investigated as-cast, CA + HE and CA + MM + SPS Al-17Fe alloys and the reference cast Al-12Si-1Mg-1Ni-1Cu alloy were performed at laboratory temperature, at 400 °C and at laboratory temperature after 100 h of annealing at 400 °C. The results of the testing are shown in Fig. 5 and clearly summarized in Table 3. The investigated CA + HE and CA + MM + SPS Al-17Fe alloys demonstrated laboratory temperature (Fig. 5a) properties that were similar to or superior to the cast reference alloy. The CA + HE alloy exhibited the highest compressive yield strength (CYS) of all the tested materials, reaching 428 MPa, with an ultra-high ductility of approximately 34%. However, the CA + MM + SPS alloy exhibited a lack of ductility. Both the investigated alloys showed satisfactory compressive strengths (CSs) of 637 and 550 MPa for the CA + HE and CA + MM + SPS alloys, respectively. The results were similar to those for the reference cast Al-12Si-1Cu-1Mg-1Ni alloy, which had a CS and CYS of 680 and 384 MPa, respectively. In comparison, the as-cast Al-17Fe alloy showed low CS of 102 MPa and CYS of 91 MPa mainly due to the presence of large and brittle intermetallic phases.

The un-notched specimens were round with gauge length of 30 mm and diameter of 8 mm. The total specimen length was 100 mm. The AlSi7Mg0.3 samples were heat-treated using T6 tempering and had tensile strength Rm = 272 MPa; yield strength Rp0.2 = 194 MPa; elongation at fracture A = 8.7% and Brinell hardness of 93 HB. As indicated in , fatigue experiments were carried-out on the specimens by using a 25 kN servo-hydraulic machine at room temperature. Under constant-amplitude axial loading, the stress-controlled tests were conducted at stress ratio R = −1 and frequency f = 80 Hz.

The patterns of elasticity were similar to those of the tensile strength. PAN-0 showed an elasticity of 9.0% and PAN-2 showed an increased elasticity of 14%. However, further increases in the kaolin content decreased the elasticity—except for PAN-10. When the kaolin dose was 2%, its dispersion in the polymer was uniform, as shown in the SEM images, thus making a strong interconnection that allows for an enhanced mechanical strength and elasticity of the membranes . 文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Another important precipitate hardening stainless steel, identified in recent research for its applicability to additive manufacturing, is CX stainless steel. This material was recently developed and commercially produced as a powder by EOS GmbH . CX stainless steel parts produced using LPBF can exhibit superior toughness to LPBF 17–4 PH and 316L steels in the as-built condition, perhaps paving the way for the replacement of these steels with CX in certain applications . Thermal aging treatments performed at 530 °C for 3 h result in the formation of rod-/needle-shaped NiAl-based nanoprecipitates within the martensitic matrix both with and without a preceding solutionising heat treatment . With these heat treatments, LPBF-produced CX stainless steel can be fabricated with UTS of up to 1601 MPa . Due to the relative lack of literature discussing it, CX steel will not be discussed further in the following section.

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In this paper, an experimental study is presented on an investigation to improve the weld strength of laser-welded joints via post-processing by warm laser shock peening (wLSP). A dual-laser setup was utilized to simultaneously heat the sample to a prescribed temperature and to perform the wLSP process on the laser-welded joints of AA6061-T6 and TZM alloys. Joints in overlap and bead-on-plate configurations were created by laser welding by a high-power fiber laser and post-processed with wLSP. The tensile tests carried out on wLSP-processed AA6061-T6 samples demonstrate an enhancement in the strength by about 20% over as-welded samples and the ductility of samples processed by wLSP improved by 30% over as-welded samples. The bead-on-plate (BOP) welds of TZM alloy processed with wLSP demonstrated an enhancement in strength by about 30% and the lap welds processed with wLSP demonstrated an increase in the joint strength by 22%. Finite element analysis revealed that the depth and magnitude of compressive stresses imparted by wLSP were greater than room temperature laser shock peening (rtLSP), which contributed to the enhancement of the joint strength for processed samples.
DOI:10.1007/s00170-019-03868-y

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The yield strength significantly increased up to the volume fraction of 10%, and afterwards, it reached an asymptotic value of approximately 220 MPa. The enhancement of mechanical properties due to the Al62Cu26Fe12 particles can be better appreciated by considering the increment of yield stress. For a commercial Al composites reinforced by 10 vol.% of either SiC or Al2O3 particles, the increase of yield stress varies from approximately 50–110 MPa, depending on the matrix, particle size, and heat treatment conditions. For the present Al/(10%AlCuFe)p composites, the average increase of yield stress was about 150 MPa without any heat treatment. This indicates the relatively efficient strengthening provided by the quasicrystalline and coexisting crystalline phases in the Al62Cu26Fe12 particles. Also it should be mentioned that the increase in strength in the composites can be partially attributed to the matrix strengthening due to dissolution of small satellite powders, as shown in Fig. 2(b).

问题:Which compound has a yield strength of 1312 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The aforementioned inverse optimization for both 25 °C and 1000 °C starting from AlMo0.5NbTa0.5TiZr constitutes just one example of workflow that has been developed to improve upon a base alloy. The choice of the AlMo0.5NbTa0.5TiZr RHEA to serve as the base alloy was informed by its already having an exceptional high-temperature yield strength. Other rationales could be used that could lead one to choose a different known alloy as a starting point for the optimization. For example, in addition to yield strength, ductility is a mechanical property of frequent concern. Yet, yield strength and ductility have a well-known tradeoff, where increasing ductility tends to lead to lower yield strength (Supplementary Fig. 12). Thus, it is logical to start with compositions that already possess a good room temperature ductility and manipulate them to maximize yield strength through optimization. While there is no certainty that the discovered compositions with improved yield strengths will retain their ductility, ML models can be developed to maximize both yield strength and ductility simultaneously. Such models are currently under development by us where one can find compositions that simultaneously improve multiple properties. In this paper, we have chosen two additional base alloys, Mo0.3NbTiV0.3Zr and HfNbTaTiZr, which show high room temperature ductility. Mo0.3NbTiV0.3Zr has a moderate yield strength at 25 °C (1312 MPa) and a ductility of 49.3%. HfNbTaTiZr also has a high ductility (33.3%) but a very poor yield strength at 25 °C (929 MPa).
DOI:10.1038/s41524-022-00926-0_

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Segmentation of Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine (DICOM) images with Mimics Innovation Suite 22 was performed on computed tomography (CT) scans for the implants, which were designed using Geomagics Sculpt 2019.2.50 (3D Systems). This resulted in surface tessellation language (STL) files of the maxillofacial complex designed by CADskills. Autodesk Meshmixer 3.5.474 and Materialise 3-matic 14.0 were used to process and remesh the parts, respectively, using C3D10M mesh elements. A convergence study was performed to determine an appropriate mesh density, taking the tradeoff between the accuracy of the results and the computational load into account. A mesh density was chosen for which the average von Mises stress values did not deviate more than 4.4% from the converged value. Two different meshes were created, one for a Cawood and Howell class V patient and one for a Cawood and Howell class VIII patient. In the latter, the maxillary component was further resorbed based on the first mesh using Autodesk Meshmixer. The volume meshes were exported to the FEA software Abaqus/CAE 2017. Table 1 shows the different material properties assigned to the different components in the assembly. All materials were assumed to be linear elastic and isotropic. Titanium Grade 23 (TiAl6V4) ELI was used for both AMSJI subunits and the connector, with yield strength σy = 1116 MPa and ultimate strength σt = 1286 MPa (Table 1).

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of A360?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Recently additive manufacturing of aluminum alloys by selective laser melting (SLM) is of research interest due to its potential benefits in manufacturing industries. The present work investigates the SLM-built AlSi10Mg thin-walled parts and their macro-mechanical behavior in correlation with relative densities. The superlative mechanical behavior of SLM parts was achieved by using the optimal parameters, i.e. 320 W of laser power, 900 mm/s of scanning speed, and 80 μm of hatch distance. The results showed that the SLMed AlSi10Mg thin-walled specimens attained the highest relative density of 99.86 % and 99.21 % for the wall thickness of 0.50 mm and 5.0 mm, respectively. For 0.50 mm wall thickness specimen, the tensile strength of 250.9 MPa, yield strength of 143.7 MPa, breakage elongation of 5.31 %, and microhardness of 116.8 HV were attained which would be comparable to those of the conventionally die-cast A360 alloy. The 364 MPa of tensile strength and 12.04 % of breakage were achieved for 5.0 mm thin-walled specimen. The fracture behavior of different thin-walled fabricated tensile specimens was also examined using a scanning electron microscope (SEM).
DOI:10.1016/j.jmapro.2021.04.022

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of S32654?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The stress-strain curves, tensile strength and elongation of all the tensile samples are plotted in Fig. 13. During tensile tests, all the tensile samples underwent four typical stages including elastic deformation, plastic deformation, work hardening and fracture (Fig. 13a). The stages of plastic deformation and work hardening are more pronounced. Compared with the BM sample, the work hardening phenomena of two joints are more obvious in the plastic deformation process. In addition, the higher the rotational speed, the more significant the work hardening phenomenon is. The BM sample has an ultimate tensile strength (UTS) and yield strength (YS) of 875 and 445 MPa with an elongation of 65.2%, indicating that S32654 exhibits superior strength and excellent ductility. After FSW at 300 rpm, the UTS and YS values are 890 and 505 MPa, respectively. For the sample at 400 rpm, the two values are 895 and 510 MPa. This reveals that raising rotational speed leads to a slight increase in YS and UTS. However, the elongations of tensile samples at 300 and 400 rpm are reduced from 65.2% (BM sample) to 41.7 and 37.2%, respectively, which suggest that the plastic deformation capacities of joints decrease. Moreover, the SEM fractography exhibits apparent necking (Fig. 13c) and dimples with a microvoid coalescence feature (Fig. 13d), which reveals a typical characteristic of ductile fracture after FSW.
DOI:10.1016/j.matdes.2017.01.034
