问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 595 kPa for Fe3O4?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):To evaluate the contribution of PPy and Fe3O4 NPs to the mechanical properties of the PVA hydrogel, the tensile tests of the pure PVA, PPy/PVA, and Fe3O4/PPy/PVA hydrogels were performed. As shown in Fig. 3, for the PPy/PVA hydrogel, it was clear that the tensile strength, breaking strain increased to be 211 kPa, 275%, respectively, which were higher than that of PVA hydrogel (169 kPa, 224%). These results showed that the fabrication of PPy could effectively improve the mechanical properties of the PVA hydrogel. More importantly, after in-situ precipitation of Fe3O4 NPs within the PPy/PVA hydrogel network, the maximum tensile strength of the Fe3O4/PPy/PVA hydrogel enhanced to be 595 kPa which was 3.5 times higher than pure PVA hydrogel (Fig. 3a). At the same time, the elastic modulus of the Fe3O4/PPy/PVA hydrogel reached 461 ± 25 kPa (Fig. 3b), which was 16.5 times higher than PVA hydrogel. Furthermore, the toughness of the Fe3O4/PPy/PVA hydrogel also dramatically increased (Fig. 3c). These indicated that Fe3O4 NPs within the hydrogel served as nano-reinforcing particles [] and improved the mechanical properties of the PPy/PVA conductive hydrogel. Therefore, we concluded that the introduction of PPy and Fe3O4 NPs into PVA hydrogel network by the in-situ method facilitated great enhancement of the mechanical strength, elasticity modulus, and toughness of the hydrogel.
DOI:10.1016/j.matdes.2020.108759

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 410 MPa for TiAl?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Fig. 6 compares the tensile strength and total elongation at fracture of the as-sintered and as-forged TiAl composites at 900 °C. It can be seen that, in the sintered conditions, the ultimate tensile strength (σUTS) and yield strength (σy) are 410 MPa and 312 MPa, respectively, with a total elongation at fracture (δ) of 12.10%. After forging, σUTS and σy decreased slightly, with 384 MPa and 291 MPa, respectively. Although there was slight decrease of the strength after forging, these are much higher than those of the conventional binary TiAl alloys and high niobium containing γ-TiAl-based alloys prepared by powder metallurgical processing . The high strength of the composite is mainly due to the reinforcing effect of the stiff Ti2AlC particles and the solid solution strengthening of the Nb element. It is well known that the onset of plastic deformation in the TiAl alloy and composites represents a deformation mechanism of dislocation activity, such as the dislocation accumulation, the dislocation multiplication and the interactions of dislocations in the primary slip system and intersecting slip system . Thus, the fine Ti2AlC particles would hinder the dislocations slip during tensile process. The Ti2AlC/TiAl composites possess a larger capacity for dislocation storage compared with TiAl alloy . Thus, the onset of necking deformation of the Ti2AlC/TiAl composites can be postponed, which contributes to the improvement of tensile strength. In addition, it is worthwhile to note that δ increases significantly, with high δ up to 30.87%, roughly doubled higher than elongation in the sintering state. According to the above analysis result of the microstructure evolution, the improvement of the ductility can be attributed to the grain refinement of the TiAl matrix and the more homogenous distribution of the Ti2AlC particles.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2014.06.009

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):PM304 coating on a Ni-based superalloy rod for high temperature lubrication. PM304 coating on a Ni-based superalloy rod for high temperature lubrication has been prepared by high-energy ball milling and powder metallurgy techniques. The composition of the PM304 coating is the same as that of PS304 coating, but the microstructure is quite different. The microstructure of PM304 coating is fine and dense; the size of self-lubricating particles in the coating is very small. Self-lubricating Cr2O3 particles are about 100 nm, BaF2CaF2 particles about 1 μm, Ag particles below 5 μm, while BaF2/CaF2 and Ag particles precipitated from NiCr matrix are less than 50 nm. The fine and dense microstructure results in increased tensile strength and crack growth resistance of PM304 coating. The mean tensile strength is about 46 MPa.

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 14.5% for Mg-8.2Gd-3.8Y-1.0Zn-0.4Zr?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Nowadays, researchers have been trying to enhance the mechanical properties of Mg-RE-Zn alloys by composition and/or by processing technologies. Xu et al. fabricated a Mg-8.2Gd-3.8Y-1.0Zn-0.4Zr alloy sheet by virtue of vertical direct chill casting, extrusion, hot rolling and peak-aging treatment. This sheet exhibited ultra-high strength (tensile yield stress of 416 MPa and ultimate tensile strength of 505 MPa) and high ductility (elongation to failure of 12.8%). Furthermore, Xu et al. tailored the strength (tensile yield stress of 466 MPa) and ductility (elongation to failure of 14.5%) of Mg-8.2Gd-3.8Y-1.0Zn-0.4Zr alloy by means of hot extrusion, followed by forced-air cooling and an artificial aging treatment. In addition, Yu et al. successfully fabricated a Mg-11Gd-4.5Y-1Nd-1.5Zn-0.5Zr alloy via hot extrusion, cold rolling and aging treatment. The optimal mechanical properties obtained in their study are 502 MPa with regard to tensile yield stress and 546.8 MPa as for ultimate tensile strength. Yamada et al. developed a Mg-11Gd-4Y-1.7Zn-0.5Zr alloy by applying solution treatment, hot rolling and artificial aging treatment. They reported that this material possesses a tensile yield stress of ∼365 MPa and an ultimate tensile strength of ∼460 MPa. It is worth noting that hot deformation has been termed as an essential prerequisite for the fabrication of Mg-RE-Zn alloys with excellent mechanical properties. Therefore, for the purpose of alloy development and engineering applications, it is imperative to obtain a comprehensive understanding on the hot deformation behavior of Mg-RE-Zn alloys.
DOI:10.1016/j.jma.2021.07.008

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of Beryl?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Figure 11 shows graph of UTS versus varying wt% of beryl particles. Tensile strength of As-cast composite increases with increasing beryl wt% up to 6%, and after that it decreases. The actual value observed is 168 MPa for 0% beryl and 186 MPa for 6% wt of beryl content, suggesting that beryl particles influence the improvement in tensile strength of the matrix up to 6% wt. The above is understandable since dislocation density has grown due to the difference between the coefficient of thermal expansion of matrix and reinforcement. Higher energy is required to maneuver the dislocation in the grain boundaries. Hard ceramic particles inhibit the promotion of dislocation front, substantiating the matrix. The increase in the strength may also be the result of the compactness of reinforcement and smaller interparticle spacing in the matrix. Due to internal ductility of the material, localized internal stress is developed. It can be noticed that composite containing 6% wt beryl particles revealed higher strength. The addition of the particles beyond this brings down the strength. A decrease is ascribed to agglomeration and apartheid of the particles toward the eutectic phase. The agglomerated particles show a fragile area in the structure, which may act as a ‘crack initiator’ and lowers the strength developed because of agglomerated particles. The decrease in tensile strength for 6 wt% beryl reinforced aluminum is because of the effect of segregation of beryl particle. The tensile strength of Al–SiCp for 6% wt of SiCp is 180 Mpa, and for Al2024–beryl for 6% wt of beryl it is 186 Mpa which is little higher .

问题:Which compound has a ultimate tensile strength of 333.7 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):This paper investigated the influences of Mg level (0.3 wt%-0.6 wt%) and heat treatment process on the phase transformation of Mg-Si phase and Fe-rich intermetallics of low pressure die-cast Al-7Si-xMg alloys. Their corresponding mechanical properties were also studied. The results indicated that at as-cast state, the addition of Mg element accelerated the formation of π-Fe phase while the mechanical properties decreased correspondingly. During the heat treatment process, the transformation of Mg-Si phase and π-Fe intermetallics were determined by the Mg content. In the alloys with low level of 0.3 wt%∼0.45 wt% Mg, the diffusion of Mg element from π-Fe phase into α-Al matrix prompted the formation of more Mg2Si strengthening phase, which was beneficial to the improvement of tensile properties. However, in the alloys with high level of 0.6 wt% Mg, the diffusion kinetic of Mg element from π-Fe phase into α-Al matrix was repressed due to the saturated Mg in the matrix. The π-Fe phase consumed large amount of Mg element and facilitated crack initiation which definitely weakened the strengthening effect. The mechanical properties, especially the ductility, were strongly deteriorated. In this study, the best comprehensive mechanical properties were obtained from the Al-7Si-0.45 Mg alloy, which was 267.3 MPa and 333.7 MPa, 9% for YS, UTS and Elongation, respectively.
DOI:10.1016/j.jallcom.2019.01.352

问题:Which compound has a ultimate tensile strength of 515.5 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Titanium matrix composites (TMC) have potential applications in the aerospace industry because of their excellent performance. The comprehensive performance of TMC mainly depends on the matrix, reinforcement and interface characteristics. Hence, this study discussed the knowledge of microstructure-property relationships in detail. Crack-free high-mass-proportion TiCp/Ti6Al4Vcomposites were successfully prepared by directed energy deposition (DED). As the TiCp mass fraction increasing from 0 to 50%, the quantity of primary TiC and unmelted TiC (UMT) increased. Meanwhile, the refined α-Ti in the composites had a relatively weak texture. In addition, the interface between primary TiC and α-Ti was a semi-coherent interface, exhibiting a 112-0 α-Ti // [110]TiC, 1-100 α-Ti // 1-11 TiC orientation relationship, which facilitated the heterogeneous nucleation of Ti and improved bonding of primary TiC with the matrix. With the increase in microhardness taking the form of a cubic function, the wear mechanism was found to transform from abrasive wear to slight delamination wear. Due to the fact that both UMT and primary TiC bonded well with Ti64 matrix, they shared partial friction to protect matrix from severe abrasion, resulting in an excellent wear resistance of composites. Moreover, the thermal conductivity of 50% TiCp/Ti6Al4V was 9.063 W∙m-1∙K-1, which was nearly 26.5% higher than that of Ti6Al4V. Owing to the premature cracking of brittle UMT and dendritic TiC, the tensile strength and elongation of the composite with 50% TiCp were 515.5 MPa and 1.83%, which decreased by 45.8% and 78.8%, respectively. Adding a high proportion of TiCp can significantly improve the hardness and wear resistance of TMC, whereas it is detrimental to the tensile performance of TMC. The study have significant implications for the design of novel TMC, particularly for the aerospace industrial applications.
DOI:10.1016/j.addma.2020.101323

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of Zr41.2Ti13.8Cu12.5 Ni10Be22.5?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The Zr41.2Ti13.8Cu12.5Ni10Be22.5 (Vitreloy 1) bulk metallic glass exhibits an exceptional glass forming ability with a critical cooling rate of ∼1 K/s, which makes it a very interesting material for structural applications. This has motivated a series of investigations of its mechanical properties . The Vitreloy 1 alloy shows a tensile strength of 1.9 GPa, and an elastic strain limit of 2% under compressive or tensile loading. Plain strain fracture toughness measurements on Vitreloy 1 have revealed values between K1C=18–55 MPa m1/2, depending on purity and residual stresses of the tested material. However, Vitreloy 1, as all other metallic glasses, fails by the formation of highly localized shear bands, which leads to catastrophic failure under unconstrained conditions without much macroscopic plasticity . This quasi-brittle deformation behavior has limited the application of bulk metallic glasses as an engineering material so far. The preparation of bulk metallic glass matrix composites with ductile metal and refractory ceramic particles as reinforcements has yielded improvements in tensile and compressive strains to failure . Multiple shear bands are observed when the catastrophic instability is avoided by mechanical constrains. In this paper we present a new ductile metal reinforced bulk metallic glass material prepared via a cost effective in situ processing method . This composite exhibits large compressive and tensile strains to failure and a strongly improved impact toughness compared to other metallic glass materials.
DOI:10.1016/S1359-6454(01)00068-4

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 665 MPa for 316L?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In this study, the layer thickness in the Selective Laser Melting (SLM) process was increased to 200 μm to solve the problem of the low build rate of SLM. And adopted the heat treatment process to improve the mechanical properties of specimens. The single-tracks and multi-layer fabrication experiments of the aerosolized 316L stainless steel powder were carried out by using 400 W laser power. The process parameters were optimized by analyzing the density, the microstructure, the tensile properties and the defect mechanism of the molded parts. The experiment results showed that when the exposure time was 120–160 μs and the overlap rate was 30–50%, the relative densities of the samples could be higher, up to 99.99%. The un-melted defects, the balling defects, and the micropore defects were observed in the SLM process. Un-melted defects can be avoided by adjusting the distance between the points and the hatch spacing. Balling included large balling and spatter balling. The large balling can be completely avoided by adjusting the process parameters. The spatter balling was divided into droplet spatter and powder spatter, which cannot be completely eradicated but can be inhibited by decreasing the exposure time. The build rate can be enhanced to 12.4 mm3/s. By the heat treatment process, the yield strength, the tensile strength and the elongation of the sample can reach 665 MPa, 718 MPa, and 35%, respectively.
DOI:10.1016/j.optlastec.2020.106872

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of MP35N?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In lead wire applications for medical devices, cyclic deformation is more important than monotonic deformation. An examination of prior literature on the cyclic deformation of MP35N alloy shows that few detailed studies have been published to-date. For example, Smith and Yates reported an endurance limit of 1000 MPa for an MP35N alloy in bar form with a tensile strength of 1800 MPa using tension–tension fatigue (TTF) tests with a stress ratio R of 0.1. MP35N in wire form has also been studied previously in fatigue using rotating beam bending fatigue tests (RBBF; R=−1). Scheiner et al. studied the fatigue properties of small diameter MP35N alloy wires (d~50 μm) by RBBF tests and documented that two different MP35N alloy wires with tensile strengths of 1770 MPa and 2170 MPa exhibited similar fatigue life cycles at any given stress but with large scatter in cyclic life at low stresses. Altman et al. also reported similar RBBF response in MP35N alloy wires with a diameter of ~100–130 μm with 500 MPa as an endurance limit. Hildebrand et al. observed wire diameter size effect on the fatigue limit of MP35N alloy material; the smaller the diameter of the wire, the lower was the endurance limit – for example, it was 460 MPa for ~180 μm whereas it was ~330 MPa for ~130 μm wires. While the RBBF test is representative of conditions that prevail in a lead wire in service (bending component), TTF tests provide a more direct relation of cyclic deformation response to microstructure.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2014.05.046

问题:Which compound has a ultimate tensile strength of 29.01 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Mechanical strength is an important fundamental property which determines the quality of a film developed. The measured values of the tensile strength (TS), elongation at break (EB) and Young modulus (Y) of the films are shown in Table 4. As can be noticed, the mechanical properties of the edible films under study were greatly influenced by the incorporation of nano-TiO2. On comparing pure starch-pectin and nano-TiO2 incorporated films it was found that the addition of nano-titania has drastically increased the TS value in the latter case by about 29.8% (Table 4). To be specific, the TS value of the control film was enhanced significantly and reached a maximum value of 29.01 MPa when the nano-TiO2 amount is increased from 0 to 4% wt (p < 0.05). This could be explained by the fact that, the incorporation of nano-titania into the film solutions effectively increased the interfacial interactions within the control film network via the establishment of hydrogen and covalent bonds between TiO2 and starch molecules. Consequently, the starch molecular chain rigidity was higher, which could positively influence the strength of nanocomposite films. Moreover, a substantial increase in the elastic modulus (Y) of the films could be observed with the addition of nano-TiO2. Typically, the Y value becomes nearly doubled when the nano-TiO2 concentration was increased from 0 to 4%. This is probably due to the weak interfacial interaction between the polymer matrix and nano-TiO2. Contrary to the tensile strength and young modulus, a gradual decrease from 12.96% to 6.14% occurred as for the elongation at break (EB) of the starch-based films without and with nano inclusions. This behavior is likely due to the phenomenon known as anti-plasticization. Moreover, nano-TiO2 may act as an anti-plasticizer by reducing the free volume between the biopolymer chains, and decreasing the flexibility of the films by way of filling and mutual interaction [].

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):To evaluate the correlation between the mechanical properties and microstructure of the ECAPed AZ91 alloy, all samples were subjected to tensile tests at room temperature. Fig. 15(a)–(c) shows the engineering stress-strain curves of the as-cast, homogenized, and ECAPed samples at 593 K, 563 K and 533 K from one to eight passes. As can be observed, the AZ91 alloy in cast condition shows a low strength and ductility. After homogenization the yield strength value was slightly decreased while an enhancement was seen in ductility. According to the results, ECAP processing at 593 K led to a remarkable improvement in strength and ductility of the homogenized AZ91 alloy (see Fig. 15(a)). The variation of mechanical properties with the number of ECAP passes is given in Fig. 16. At 593 K, as the strain induced with the ECAP increased, the ultimate strength and elongation to failure were enhanced. After eight passes, the ultimate strength (302 MPa) was about twofold the as-cast sample (147 MPa) and elongation to failure was significantly increased from 2.5% (cast condition) to 18.0% (8 passes). The yield strength of the sample ECAPed at 593 K was increased up to the second pass but a drop could be distinguished after four and eight passes. However, after the eighth pass, the yield strength (152 MPa) was still higher than that in initial condition (84 MPa). A descending trend was found for the yield strength of samples ECAPed at 533 K after the second pass while a relatively high yield strength (188 MPa) was obtained at 563 K with increasing the ECAP passes up to eight. The lower ECAP temperature resulted in higher value of yield strength after four ECAP passes while the sample ECAPed at 533 K showed the minimum value after the sixth pass (see Fig. 16(b)).

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):High as-cast strength die-cast AlSi9Cu2Mg alloy was achieved with industrially acceptable ductility, via the addition of titanium diboride nanoparticles. The main intermetallic compounds in the die-cast AlSi9Cu2Mg alloy were identified as θ–Al2Cu and Q–Al5Cu2Mg8Si6 phases. The Fe-rich compound formed in the present die-cast alloy with a high Mn/Fe ratio of 3:1 was determined as the BCC structured α–Al15(Fe,Mn)3Si2 phase with a lattice parameter of a = 1.2702 nm, and the Fe-rich compound had highly faceted morphology with {110} surface termination. The Al matrix phase in the die-cast AlSi9Cu2Mg alloy was refined by ∼65% from 18.1 μm to 6.4 μm, after adding 3.0 wt% titanium diboride nanoparticles. The titanium diboride nanoparticles in the alloy showed coherent interface with the Al matrix, with the (11-1) lattice face of Al parallel to the (0001) lattice face of titanium diboride. The as-cast AlSi9Cu2Mg alloy containing 3.0 wt% titanium diboride nanoparticles exhibited the high yield strength of 233 ± 3 MPa and the tensile strength of 398 ± 7 MPa, as well as the industrially acceptable elongation of 4.8 ± 0.7%, and it showed at least 23% improvement in the as-cast yield strength over the existing die-cast Al alloys. The strengthening by nanoparticle and coherent interface (∼32 MPa) was higher than that by the refining of Al matrix (∼10 MPa). The addition of titanium diboride nanoparticles also affected the fracture of the die-cast AlSi9Cu2Mg alloy, and cracks were prone to form in the Q and Fe-rich compounds, after the addition of titanium diboride nanoparticles.
DOI:10.1016/j.jallcom.2020.156873

The yield strength of 0.1 wt% GNPs/Cu composites and 0.2 wt% GNPs/Cu composites were 159 MPa and 171 MPa respectively. Accordingly, the yield strength was increased by 26.1% and 35.7% compared to that of pure copper (126 MPa). However, the yield strength of 0.3 wt% GNPs/Cu composites was reduced to 117 MPa. The tensile strength increased from 183 MPa to 214 MPa by adding 0.1 wt% of GNP into the composites. The tensile strength of 0.2 wt% GNPs/Cu composites was 233 MPa, 27% more compared to pure copper. Despite the fact that GNPs is not a single-layer graphene, it can still increase the tensile strength of copper matrix composites for its unique two-dimensional flake structure and high surface area. 文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In the present study, a homogeneous fine grained (∼6 μm) AZ75 alloy with high strength and ductility simultaneously was produced by HPR. The microstructure, strengthening mechanism and superplasticity of the AZ75 alloy were investigated. The microstructure analysis revealed that the alloy exhibited a partial recrystallized microstructure with numerous micron-/nano– Mg17Al12 precipitates uniformly dispersed both at grain boundaries and within grain interiors. The HPRed AZ75 alloy exhibited both a high strength and high ductility at room temperature, e.g. YS of ∼218 MPa, UTS of ∼357 MPa and elongation of ∼19%. The predominant mechanism for high YS of the alloy is found to be grain boundary strengthening. The high ductility is attributed to the strong working hardening ability benefited from Zn solid solutes and the presence of numerous well–dispersed nanosized precipitates as well as a weakened basal texture in the alloy. Moreover, the HPRed AZ75 alloy exhibited an optimum superplasticity with a maximum tensile elongation of ∼615% at 1 × 10−3 s−1 at 300 °C. It is attributed to the enhanced GBS, as a stable microstructure with fine grain size was well maintained during tension, due to the pinning effect by numerous Mg17(Al, Zn)12 particles segregating along grain boundaries.
DOI:10.1016/j.jallcom.2017.11.348

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The variation of microstructure and mechanical properties along the cross section of squeeze cast Al-5Mg-3Zn-1Cu-1Si alloy is studied under both squeeze-cast and peak-aged condition (470 °C × 24 h + 125 °C × 24 h). For squeeze-cast alloy, along the depth direction, second dendrite arm space and the percentage of α-Al increase significantly, while the volume fraction of T-Mg32(AlZnCu)49 and Mg2Si phase showed a dramatic decrease. The ultimate tensile strength of squeeze-cast alloy slightly decreased from 357.04 MPa for the surface layer to 344.21 MPa for the central layer, yield strength dropped from 296.86 MPa to 241.03 MPa, but the elongation almost tripled from 1.42% to 3.78%. After peak aging, the ultimate tensile strength of surface layer and central layer reached 538.45 MPa and 504.03 MPa, yield strength reached 447.46 MPa and 442.65 MPa, and the elongation reached 5.23% and 3.97% respectively. Meanwhile the difference of ultimate tensile strength and elongation was increased, but that of yield strength reduced after aging treatment. Precipitation strengthening is the main strengthening mechanism while the improvement in ductility is due to the dissolution of T-Mg32(AlZnCu)49 and the morphological change of eutectic Mg2Si.

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Hardness of AM FCC MPEAs such as CoCrFeNi, CoCrFeNiMn, and Al0.5CoCrFeNi vary from 200 to 400 HV . Introducing BCC phases or secondary particles by adding Al, C, B and Ti can generally increase the hardness in AM MPEAs (Fig. 15a) . Refractory MPEAs, either fabricated using AM or conventional manufacturing methods, often exhibit higher hardness due to their BCC structure and the lattice disorder caused by larger atoms (e.g., Ta and W). For example, the hardness of DED fabricated TiZrNbWMo was 700 HV, and it increased to 1300 HV after annealing at 800 °C for 20 h . In addition, tensile properties of AM MPEAs can also be influenced dramatically by the addition or removal of components. The quinary CoCrFeMnNi produced by PBF exhibited a yield strength of 510 MPa, an ultimate strength of 610 MPa, and an elongation of 36% . In contrast, quaternary MPEAs CoCrFeNi fabricated by using the same AM technique had a yield strength, an ultimate tensile strength and an elongation of 600 MPa, 745 MPa and 32%, respectively .
DOI:10.1007/s10853-022-06961-y

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The 14YWT samples investigated are nanostructured ferritic alloys. They contain ultrafine grains and high densities of nanometer-sized oxide particles. These nanometer-sized oxide particles are also referred to as Y-, Ti-, and O-enriched nanoclusters and provide excellent high-temperature strength and creep properties, good low-temperature fracture toughness, and high resistance to neutron irradiation. Two heats of 14YWT were examined, namely, 14YWT-CR4 and 14YWT-SM12. Their chemical compositions are listed in Table I. The 14YWT-CR4 heat was made from argon gas atomized prealloyed powders made by Crucible Research. It was extruded at 850 °C, heat-treated at 1000 °C for 1 h, and rolled parallel to the extrusion direction at 600 °C to 40% reduction in thickness (RIT). The 14YWT-SM12 heat was produced by a mechanical alloying process using gas-atomized powder produced by Special Metals Inc. It was extruded at 850 °C, annealed at 1000 °C for 1 h followed by rolling parallel to the extrusion direction at 1000 °C to 50% RIT. Due to improved mechanical alloying conditions, SM12 has significantly lower C, N, and O levels than CR4. For both heats, miniature sheet-type tensile samples were machined, with the rolling direction parallel to the loading axis. The nominal yield strength of heat SM12 is approximately 1100 MPa at room temperature and decreases continuously with increased temperature; at 800 °C, its yield strength is 250 MPa. The nominal dimensions of the gauge section of these samples were 5 mm × 1.2 mm × 0.75 mm.
DOI:10.1557/jmr.2015.50

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The FeCrAl/Zy4 bi-layer clad plate was fabricated by solid diffusion, hot rolled and annealing treatment. The interface microstructure and phase analysis, texture, shear and tensile strength of the clad plate were investigated in details. The diffusional interface of clad plate was tightly bonded, and there were no cracks and pores. The intermediate phases formed at the interface region of the plate were identified as αZrCr2, ZrFe2 and Zr3Al. In addition, Zr3Fe formed at the interface of the sample annealed at 600 °C for 3 h (A63) and the sample annealed at 600 °C for 5 h (A65). For A65 samples, the texture of FeCrAl alloy is <111>∥ND, and a small part of <101>∥RD and <101>∥TD, the texture of Zy4 alloy are mainly <0001>∥ND, and a small part of <101 ̄0>∥RD and <112 ̄0>∥RD. These textures are favorable for plastic deformation of A65 samples. The optimal annealing process is 600 °C for 5 h, the sample has good comprehensive properties, shear strength 202 MPa, tensile yield strength 169 MPa, tensile utimate strength 563 MPa and fracture elongation 29%. The shear fracture surface of Zy4 alloy in A65 sample showed a mixture of dimple and brittle features. The fracture of FeCrAl alloy in the A65 sample shows ductile feature, while the Zy4 alloy side near the interface shows mixed fracture, and the region far away from the interface shows ductile fracture. The results in this study could provide possible valuable guidance for fabricating double or multilayer fuel cladding materials for the application in the nuclear reactor.

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The precipitation behavior of second phases and mechanical property of Fe–13.5Cr–4.7Al–2.1Mo–0.5Nb–0.8Ta–0.2Zr (wt.%) alloy was investigated in the present study. Alloy ingots were fabricated by vacuum arc melting and multi-pass hot rolling, then solid-solutionized at 1473 K for 2 h and aged at 1073 K for 0–24 h. In the initial aging process, the precipitated phase were mainly composed of spherical Laves Fe2M (M = Nb, Ta, Zr) and Fe23Zr6 particles. With increasing the aging time, the morphology of Laves phase particles changed gradually from the spherical shape to short-rod shape, and then to granular shape. While the Fe23Zr6 particles still remain spherical during the whole aging process. It is closely related to the lattice misfit between precipitated phase and the BCC matrix, in which the lattice misfit of ε = 1.3% between Fe2M and BCC is remarkably larger than that (ε = 0.2%) between Fe23Zr6 and BCC, leading to a significant change in Fe2M particles. Moreover, the morphology of Fe2M particles in solutionized-aged state is also different from that in rolled-aged state. Consequently, the yield strength (σYS = 410 MPa) of the solutionized-aged alloy is much lower than that (σYS = 560 MPa) of the rolled-aged alloy, which is ascribed to that the strengthening contribution (σor = 48 MPa) induced by short-rod particles is less than that (σor = 153 MPa) of spherical particles.
DOI:10.1016/j.jmrt.2022.07.024

In the longitudinal direction the UTS, 0.2% YS and elongation of the QT650 sample are 709.4 ± 8.0 MPa, 378.9 ± 2.9 MPa and 39.7 ± 1.0%, respectively. Compared to the as-fabricated condition WAAM sample and the cast/forged NAB alloy with similar composition, smaller anisotropy and considerably higher 0.2% YS and elongation are obtained in the QT650 sample.

To consider a more unfavourable scenario, we assume the live surface load p as being uniformly distributed over the half span, and the dead surface load g as being uniformly distributed over the entire span. 问题:What is the ductility of aluminum/steel?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Fast mechanical joining processes such as Self-Pierce Riveting (SPR) and Mechanical Clinching (MC) could be also adopted for this purpose. Both these processes involve great advantages as compared to common mechanical processes including: low cycle time, no preparation requirements (e.g. drilling a pre-hole for the insertion of the fastener), easiness of automation, low cost, enhancement of the material behavior (as the processes involve strain hardening). Both self-pierce riveting [,] and clinching [,] have been extensively adopted for aluminum alloys and have been extended for joining titanium sheets. Compared to self-pierce riveting, mechanical clinching enables several advantages, e.g. MC does not require external fasteners, which involve structure weighting and increase in cost. In addition, MC requires lower joining force (almost 70% lower) than that involved in SPR. However, these joints are generally characterized by lower strength. MC joints are produced by imposing a plastic deformation of the sheets by means of a simple equipment that includes a punch, a die and eventually a sheet ejector/blank holder. As the sheets are subjected to high deformation (up to 150%), the main limitation of clinching process is represented by the ductility of the sheets (especially that placed against the punch). Nevertheless, despite this limitation, a number of materials have been joined by MC, including aluminum/steel joints [], aluminum alloys [], magnesium [], titanium [,,], copper []. Recent developments also included hybrid connections e.g. metal/plastics [,], metal/wood [] and metal/composite [] joints.
DOI:10.1016/j.jmapro.2018.09.001

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Figure 2 shows the variation of strength and ductility of AA5052 with respect to different rolling conditions. The ST samples exhibit UTS of 170 MPa and YS 152 MPa, while its ductility is 42%. The 75% CR and 75% CGR samples possess UTS of 278 and 286 MPa, YS of 265 and 274 MPa, respectively, while their ductility reduced to 10 and 7.7%, respectively. Whereas the 75% CGW samples possess UTS of 321 MPa and YS of 308 MPa, while its ductility increased to 12.1%. Similarly, the hardness values corresponding to ST, CR, CGR and CGW samples are 55, 98, 112 and 128 Hv, respectively (Fig. 3). The increased strength and hardness in CR and CGR samples are attributed to accumulation of high dislocation density during cryogenic process, during which cross-slip or climb associated with dynamic recovery is effectively suppressed (Ref . The improved tensile strength, ductility and hardness in CGW sample are due to partial grain refinement and solid solution strengthening (Ref . The fine precipitates (Mg2Al3) thus formed during warm rolling along with deformed or broken secondary phase impurity particles helps in effective pile up of dislocation movement.

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Tensile testing experiment was carried out to analyze the stress behavior by applying a constant rate strain (0.5 mm/min). Stress-strain behavior is represented in Fig. 6. Neat PVDF matrix shows moderate tensile strength (∼47 MPa) and Young's Modulus (∼2.3 GPa), respectively. It was found that addition of small amount of CFO nanoparticles in PVDF matrix significantly enhanced the mechanical strength. With inclusion of PZT-CFO micro-/nano-particles in PVDF mixture, both tensile strength and Young's Modulus (53.6 MPa and 2.4 GPa, respectively) are reduced in comparison to PVDF-CFO composite film (65.8 MPa and 2.9 GPa respectively) but the values are still higher than PVDF film alone. Development of nano-crystalline regions along with micro regions in polymer matrix which is clearly visible in TEM micrographs, resulted in higher mechanical strength in both the composites. These regions pay a significant role in affecting the mechanical behaviors of materials, in particular, on stress and ductility. Koch identified three major sources that cause limited ductility in nano-crystalline materials, namely: (i) artifacts from processing (like pores), (ii) tensile instability and (iii) crack nucleation or propagation . It is very difficult to achieve nano-composite films free from artifacts that mask the mechanical behaviors. From Fig. 6, we can clearly observe that tensile strength of composites is increased but at the same time their percentage elongations decreased due to development of nano-crystalline regions in PVDF matrix on adding CFO and PZT fillers.

问题:What is the yield strength of Cu50Zr50?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Ingots of Cu50Zr50 and Cu65Zr35 were cast into a copper mold to produce 50 mm long cylindrical rods with a diameter of 1 mm. Both samples were then machined to cylindrical rods of (∅ 1 mm × h 2 mm). Prior to the quasistatic compression tests, they were kept in elastostatic compression at room temperature for 24 h under the same stress level of 1.44 GPa, which corresponds to 90.0 and 62.6% of the global yield strength (or flow stress) of Cu50Zr50 (σy ∼ 1.6 GPa) and Cu65Zr35 (σy ∼ 2.3 GPa), respectively. A strain gage was installed between the upper and lower jigs to monitor the deformation behaviors of the samples during the loading–unloading cycle. The relative changes in excess free volume created during the elastostatic compression were evaluated by differential scanning calorimetry (DSC; Perkin-Elmer DSC7, USA) at a heating rate of 40 K min−1. Since the exothermic heat associated with structural relaxation is usually very small, the DSC backgrounds were subtracted from each sample’s thermograms. The preloaded samples were then tested at room temperature under uniaxial compression at a strain rate of ∼10−4 s−1.
DOI:10.1016/j.scriptamat.2008.05.033

It can be seen from Fig. 2 that the homogenized sample possesses a low yield strength (YS) of 122 MPa but a high ductility (64%). After ECAP, the YS of the sample was increased to 466 MPa, which is almost 3 times higher than that of the homogenized sample, and a reasonable high uniform elongation (UE) of 21.3% still retains. After annealing, an abnormal increasement in strength is observed accompanied by slightly decreased ductility. Detailly, the yielding strength, ultimate tensile strength and the uniform elongation of the ECAP-annealing sample are 565 MPa, 630 MPa, and 18.4%, respectively. Fig. 3 shows the comparison of the mechanical properties of the brass in this work and reported in references [,]. It is evident that the Cu-38Zn alloy processed by either conventional deformation or SPD undergoes strength-ductility trade-off. The present ECAP and ECAP-annealing samples exhibit high strength-ductility synergy, and thus, jump out of the so-called ‘banana curve’ region.

The PCL scaffold depicted a soft nature and moderate flexibility with its tensile strength of 25.14 ± 4.1 MPa. Blending ceramic materials with polymers is known to enhance the mechanical properties of the polymer matrix . In our study, the incorporation of 10 % nHA in PCL matrix caused an increase in the tensile strength of the PCL/nHA composite to 38.13 ± 2.4 MPa. Further, it was observed that the addition of ZnO nanoparticles caused a concentration-dependent decrease in mechanical properties of the scaffolds with, PCL/nHA/ZnO- 1%, PCL/nHA/ZnO- 5%, PCL/nHA/ZnO- 10%, PCL/nHA/ZnO- 15% and PCL/nHA/ZnO- 30% exhibiting tensile strength of 14.40 ± 0.47 MPa, 14.28 ± 2.5 MPa, 13.84 ± 1.65 MPa, 10.2 ± 0.08 MPa, and 8.51 ± 0.48 MPa respectively. Similar such findings have been reported in previous studies where the addition of ZnO caused a decrease in tensile strength .

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):With insitu 3 PB test in SEM observation [], critical flexural stress of shear-bands initiation for Ti40Zr25Ni3Cu12Be20 BMG beam with 0.85-mm thickness was determined in a range of 1.99–2.29 GPa, which is about 1.16–1.34 times as high as the tensile strength of 1.71 GPa []. With electrochemical technique, Grell et al. [] studied the shear-banding initiation of Vit105 BMG in terms of passive film breakdown, in which the 3 PB test was conducted in exposure to corrosive solution of 0.01 M Na2SO4. It was shown that the flexural stress of shear bands initiation is determined to be 2.24 GPa, which is 1.35 times as high as its tensile strength of 1.66 GPa []. Consequently, a finding that the flexural stress of shear banding is slightly higher than uniaxial tensile strength seems the all cases for BMG materials. Nevertheless, the reason responsible for such a difference is lack of understanding. Simply, the negative stress gradient in bending was suggested to be a source []. However, it is arguable that the negative stress gradient only played a role of suppressing the unstable propagation of shear bands, which associated with apparent global plasticity in bending, while with a less impact on initiation events of shear banding since it occurred on outermost surface of the beam sample.

问题:Which compound has a ductility of 86%?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Fig. 7 summarizes the tensile mechanical properties of Ti-10V-2Fe-3Al specimens with solution treatment and cold rolling deformation, including ultimate strength (σb), yield strength (σ0.2), ductility (δ) and reduction of area (ψ). The ultimate strength and yield strength of the specimens increase linearly with the increasing cold rolling deformation, and the ductility as well as the reduction of area decreases monotonously with the increasing deformation. Especially, by comparison with the solution treated specimen without deformation, the cold rolling deformation of 36% raises the ultimate strength by 59% and deteriorates the ductility by 86%. In addition, cold rolling deformation causes the change in Young's modulus of Ti-10V-2Fe-3Al alloy in some extent. Table 3 presents the Young's modulus and the product of strength and elongation of Ti-10V-2Fe-3Al specimens with solution treatment and cold rolling deformations. Cold rolled specimens perform lower Young's modulus than solution treated specimen, and rising deformation from 20% to 36% leads to a mild increase of Young's modulus. Moreover, the products of strength and elongation decrease drastically with the increasing deformation, indicating the degradation of toughness in the specimens after severe cold rolling deformation.
DOI:10.1016/j.matchar.2019.109789

Thus, considering the modest 5 vol.% reinforcement and comparing with the high temperature tensile strength of the composites with a homogeneous microstructure , the increment is rather significant. This improvement can be mainly attributed to (i) the network architecture, (ii) a refined primary β grain and (iii) the branched 3D TiBw structure. 问题:Which compound has a ductility of ∼1.0%?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Following the encouraging oxidation trials on the two γ TiAl alloys they were then evaluated for the effect of extended thermal exposure on their tensile properties. To differentiate the effect of bulk microstructural changes from oxidation effects, both of the γ TiAl alloys were exposed at 700 °C for up to 1000 h as cylindrical bars, which were subsequently machined into tensile test pieces. This eliminated any surface effects. The results of this are plotted in Fig. 4. Initially it should be noted that the extruded (14:1) Ti-46-5-1 alloy exhibited excellent properties in the as-received condition with 0.2% proof strengths of ∼820 MPa, tensile strengths of ∼950 MPa and a ductility of ∼1.0%. Low levels of scatter were associated with these tensile properties; for example, a standard deviation of 10–15 MPa was found for the tensile strength, based on eight tests . Slightly lower strengths were measured for the Ti-46-5-1 alloy extruded at a ratio of 18:1; a tensile strength of ∼905 MPa and proof strength of 790 MPa. These values compare very well with other γ TiAl alloys, with only alloys like the Ti-45Al-Nb-C (TNB) family of materials offering superior strengths . The high strengths obtained with this extruded Ti-46-5-1 may be the result of several contributing factors, including the relatively fine colony size, the small lath size, and the high oxygen and nitrogen levels. Previously published tensile properties for forged Ti-46-5-1 alloy have shown proof strengths only up to ∼710 MPa .
DOI:10.1016/S0966-9795(03)00116-X

问题:Which compound has a yield strength of 926.0 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Fig. 6 and Table 3 show the tensile test results of the two alloys at room temperature and 650 °C. HIPed LPBF 625 measured yield strength (YS) of 459.5 MPa, ultimate tensile strength (UTS) of 926.0 MPa and elongation at fracture of 74.9 % at room temperature. Wrought 625 showed YS of 413.4 MPa, UTS of 914.4 MPa and elongation at fracture of 70.2 % at room temperature. In other words, the room temperature tensile properties of HIPed LPBF 625 and wrought 625 did not show significant differences. However, there were significant differences in high-temperature tensile properties (Fig. 6 (b)). While HIPed LPBF 625 measured 246.2 MPa (YS), 637.5 MPa (UTS) and 38.7 % (elongation at fracture), the wrought 625 measured 242.6 MPa (YS), 720.8 MPa (UTS) and 77.0 % (elongation at fracture). HIPed LPBF 625 alloy showed a significantly lower elongation at 650 °C compared to wrought 625. The inserted graph in Fig. 6 (b) is an enlarged section of the high-temperature tensile stress-strain curves, and serrated flows (the so-called Portevin-Le Chatelier effect) were observed in both alloys. Serrated flows occurring in Ni-based superalloys in the 450−700 °C temperature range are known to be to the result of dynamic strain aging [,]. A notable thing here is that HIPed LPBF 625 alloy shows a more frequent serrated flows compared to wrought 625.
DOI:10.1016/j.addma.2020.101377

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of SiC?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):A bulk ultrafine grained (UFG) AA6063–5 vol%SiC metal matrix nanocomposite has been synthesized by consolidation of a nanocrystalline AA6063–5 vol%SiC nanocomposite powder using two powder metallurgy (PM) routes: one combining spark plasma sintering (SPS) and hot extrusion and the other combining die pressing and hot extrusion. The yield strength, ultimate tensile strength and elongation to fracture of the sample produced by a combination of SPS and hot extrusion at 500 °C are 392 MPa, 496 MPa and 7.1%, respectively, while those of the sample produced by die pressing and hot extrusion are 449 MPa, 526 MPa and 3.5%, respectively. Pre-sintering of the powder compact using SPS prior to extrusion improves the ductility of the consolidated material without causing significant decrease of strength, suggesting that SPS has a beneficial effect on improving the consolidation level of the material. The UFG microstructure of the AA6063 matrix is the result of recrystallization of the as-milled nanocrystalline AA6063–5 vol%SiC nanocomposite powder particles during extrusion. With increasing the extrusion temperature from 500 to 550 °C, the average grain size of the consolidated sample increases from 276 nm to 627 nm, leading to a significant decrease of the yield strength from 449 to 233 MPa, but a drastic increase of the elongation to fracture from 3.5% to 9.6%.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2015.09.059

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The materials used in the production of ECC mixtures were Type-I Portland cement (C), Class-F fly ash (FA) with a lime content of 5.6%, micro silica sand with a maximum grain size of 250 μm, water, poly-vinyl-alcohol (PVA) fibers, and a polycarboxylic-ether type high-range water reducing admixture (HRWR). Chemical composition and physical properties of portland cement and FA are presented in Table 1. The PVA fibers with a diameter of 39 μm and a length of 8 mm are purposely manufactured with a tensile strength of 1620 MPa, elastic modulus of 42.8 GPa, maximum elongation of 6.0% for ECC . The PVA fiber tends to rupture instead of pull out in a cementitious matrix, as a result of the strong chemical bonding with cement due to the presence of the hydroxyl group in its molecular chains . For this reason, the surface of the PVA fibers is coated with a proprietary hydrophobic oiling agent of 1.2% by mass to tailor the interfacial properties between fiber and matrix for ECC strain-hardening performance.

问题:Which compound has a ductility of 34%?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The annealing textures and grain sizes are relatively similar for all alloys investigated. Nevertheless, a short assessment of the influence of grain size and texture for the obtained yield strength shall be made here. The grain size influences the yield strength by the Hall-Petch relation, σ=σ0+kd−12. Grain size of AX101 alloy is 34% finer compared to X01 alloy; this will lead to only 17% increase in yield strength in AX101 alloy compared to X01. Considering the Considère criterion and assuming same hardening behaviour, we calculated that 17% increase in the yield strength would decrease the uniform elongation of the alloy by 2%. These calculations show that 34% finer grain size has insignificant contribution towards strength and ductility. In terms of texture, as mentioned in the previous section, the average basal slip S-factors for all alloys are comparable which indicates that there is no significant influence of texture on mechanical properties. Nevertheless, the observed anisotropy in elongation and strength for some of the alloys may be attributed to the slight split of the basal pole intensities towards RD. Nevertheless, the variation of mechanical properties stays for all cases below 10%. Note that the textures are a result of hot rolling and recrystallization and will, therefore, not be discussed further since this paper focusses on room-temperature ductility. The above discussion indicates that the variation in mechanical properties are mostly due to intrinsic effects related to the specific alloying additions.
DOI:10.1016/j.jma.2021.03.005

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is ~154.1 MPa for Al/Al?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Fig. 5 shows the true stress and work hardening rate under the condition of various TMTs are plotted against the true strain. For comparison, mechanical properties of specimen for conventional aging process (CR + ST + aging) are also provided. As shown in Fig. 5 (a), significant strengthening was observed in three TMTs, and optimal combination of strength and ductility was obtained in ST + CR + aging process. The yield strength (YS), ultimate tensile strength (UTS) and elongation of conventional aging treatment are ~154.1 MPa, ~357.5 MPa and ~19%, respectively. The industrial applications of LMC is undermined by their relatively low yield strength for this treatment. In contrast to that, the TMT exhibited better combination of mechanical properties, i.e., strength and ductility. It is observed that the optimized effect of CR after ST is more prominent on the mechanical properties than WR and WR + CR, which exhibited a strength of 360.1 MPa and an elongation to failure of 5.9%. When the rolled specimens were aged at 120 °C for 24 h, the strength were enhanced significantly without sacrificing of ductility. It is obvious that, a combination of deformed process and ageing is more effective to improve mechanical properties of Al/Al LMCs compared with conventional aging treatment (CR + ST + aging). It is interesting to note that there is an increase in elongation by more than twice after aging treatment in ST + CR process, which is attributed to the high work hardening ability of the specimen []. As shown in Fig. 5 (b), the lower ductility in ST + CR specimen can be explained by the quick drop of work hardening rate during plastic deformation. After the aging treatment, obvious plateaus is detected in the curve of ST + CR + aging specimen which is close to the work hardening rate of conventional aging treatment, indicating excellent work hardening abilities.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2020.140313

问题:Which compound has a yield strength of 1140 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The tensile testing of DZ125L, IN718 and the welded joint after PWHT were conducted to further evaluate the effect of microstructure on the tensile properties of the welded joint. Engineering stress–strain curve of each material and the corresponding tensile properties in terms of yield strength (YS), ultimate tensile strength (UTS) and total elongation are shown in Fig. 10. The YS of the welded joint is 977 MPa and it is higher than that of the DZ125L alloy. The UTS of the welded joint is 1074 MPa, which is higher than that of the DZ125L alloy but lower than the YS of the IN718 alloy which is 1140 MPa. The joint efficiency of the welded joint was about 110% when considering the weaker DZ125L BM as the basis for comparison. The total elongation of the welded joint is around half of that of the DZ125L alloy and significantly lower than that of the IN718 alloy. 文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Figure 4a compares the tensile strength and total elongation at fracture of the microlaminated sheets at room temperature and 750 °C. The ultimate tensile strength (σUTS) and yield strength (σy) at room temperature are 235.2 MPa and 227.6 MPa, respectively, with a total elongation at fracture (δ) of ∼0.3%. At 750 °C, σUTS and σy increased to 402.4 MPa and 340.0 MPa, respectively. These are much higher than those of monolithic TiAl prepared by roll bonding and reaction annealing. Especially, the yield strength at 750 °C is about 20% higher than that reported in Ref. and even 10% higher than the yield strength at room temperature of the monolithic TiAl . At 750 °C, the total elongation at fracture increased significantly owing to the activation of additional slip systems and reached 5.72% at 750 °C, while the reported value of monolithic TiAl prepared by roll bonding and reaction annealing is less than 5% . The fracture surfaces of specimens tested at room temperature and 750 °C shown in Figure 4b and c indicate that the cracks propagate by means of interlamellar and translamellar in the fully lamellar TiAl. The overall fracture surfaces of specimens tested at room temperature are very flat, indicating a brittle type failure, while the propagation route of the crack at 750 °C is rather tortuous. In addition, the unique microlaminated structure consisting of alternating TiB2-rich and TiAl layers offers the possibility of improving the fracture toughness and maximum service temperature of the TiAl sheets, which is the subject of further investigation.
DOI:10.1016/j.scriptamat.2011.11.009

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The evolutions of microstructural variables during creep-ageing of AA2050-T34 under a tensile stress of 150 MPa were predicted according to the determined material constants, as shown in Fig. 9. The initial precipitates (r ̄d) from natural ageing of the alloy dissolve first and disappear at about 3 h of creep-ageing time, where the lowest yield strength occurs (Li et al., 2016a), as shown in Fig. 9(a). The normalised new precipitate radius (r ̄n) grows to 1 after 18 h creep-ageing time, where peak-ageing takes place. The experimental data for normalised precipitate radius obtained in Section is also plotted in Fig. 9(a), showing good agreement with the predicted results. The normalised solute concentration (c ̄) tends to decrease with creep-ageing time (Fig. 9(b)), as the slight compensation of solutes from initial dissolving precipitates has been ignored in the model. More apparent decreasing of solute concentration can be observed in the first few hours of creep-ageing time in Fig. 9(b) because the heavily accelerated nucleation of T1 precipitates occurs at the beginning (). The normalised dislocation density (ρ ̄) plays a dominant role in first two creep stages in the double primary creep feature of AA2050-T34, which lasts about 2 h. It is also predicted to saturate after 2 h of creep-ageing in this model, as shown in Fig. 9(c).

问题:Which compound has a ultimate tensile strength of 498.0 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Adding reinforcing phases is an effective method to improve the mechanical performance of high entropy alloys. The strengthening effect of single reinforcement type has thus been studied to a great extend. Here FeCrCoNi high entropy alloy matrix composites embedded with micro-scale TiC and nano-scale SiC particles were fabricated via vacuum inductive melting method. The combined volume fraction of TiC and SiC particles was 10%, while the volume fraction of each particle was varied namely, 10 : 0, 7: 3, 5 : 5, 3 :7, and 0 : 10. The results show that the composite matrix has FCC structure, and the micro- TiC and nano- SiC particles were developed in the matrix through in-situ reactions. When the volume ratio of TiC and SiC particles is 7: 3, the mechanical properties reach the optimal combination. The yield tensile strength of the composite is 498.0 MPa, which is 207.2% higher than that of the matrix. The percent elongation is 41.2%. The strength increase stems from a synergy of grain refinement, thermal mismatch, solid solution, load bearing effect, and Orowan effect.
DOI:10.1016/j.mtcomm.2020.101729

问题:What is the youngs modulus of Mg-8Al-0.3Zn-0.1Mn-0.3Ca-0.2Y?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In this study, a new high-strength, non-flammable Mg alloy was developed through the addition of small amounts of Ca and Y to the AZ80 alloy and subsequent low-temperature, low-speed indirect extrusion of the resultant alloy. The extruded Mg-8Al-0.3Zn-0.1Mn-0.3Ca-0.2Y alloy exhibited a bimodal structure consisting of fine DRXed grains and coarse non-RXed grains. It showed a very high chip ignition temperature of 820 °C and excellent mechanical properties, i.e., a TYS of 379.3 MPa, UTS of 421.7 MPa, E of 11.3%, and yield asymmetry of 0.95. The chip ignition temperature of the developed alloy improved by 200 °C in comparison to that of the commercial AZ80 alloy through the formation of double-layered protective oxides by the addition of small amounts of Ca and Y. The outstanding mechanical properties of the developed alloy are attributed to the combined effects of grain boundary strengthening by fine DRXed grains, precipitation hardening by nanosized Mg17Al12 precipitates, and strain hardening by severely deformed non-RXed grains with a strong basal texture. Under compression along the ED, no twins were observed in the DRXed grains, which had a very small size of ∼0.8 μm, whereas numerous {10–12} twins with a misorientation angle of 86° were formed in the coarse non-RXed grains. Since the TYS and yield asymmetry are strongly influenced by the microstructure—especially that of non-RXed grains—and are inversely related to each other, microstructure control should be performed carefully to achieve the desired properties.
DOI:10.1016/j.jallcom.2017.12.229

问题:Which compound has a ultimate tensile strength of 162 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The high value of the tensile strength is mostly due to the accumulation of SiC particles on the outer surface and the relatively clean, dense and defect-free surface developed by the centrifugal force. At 2.5 wt.% of particles, the measured tensile strength values were 180, 169 and 162 MPa for the outer, transition, and inner zones, respectively. While in the case of the highest weight fraction of particles (10 wt.%), the tensile strength values were 215, 187 and 165 MPa for the outer, transition, and inner zones respectively. Nevertheless, with the same intensity reached in outer and transition zones, the growth in the inner zone is not increasing. There is a similar increase rate in tensile strength in outer and transition zones. The tensile strength values showed little improvement in contrast with those for the matrix alloy due to the absence of SiCP in the inner region. On the other hand, increasing the percentage of hard particles reduces the ductility of the produced composites, as shown in Fig. 13. As the results show that the rate of elongation decreases from one zone to another according to the concentration of particles. At lowest weight fraction of particles (2.5 wt.%) of particles, the measured elongation values were 1.79, 2.3 and 2.7 % for the outer, transition, and inner zones, respectively. While in the case of the highest weight fraction of particles (10 wt.%), the tensile strength values were 1.21, 1.24 and 2.2 % for the outer, transition, and inner zones respectively. The adding of particles probably overstrained the lattice, and therefore the AZ91 matrix no longer had enough stress energy to gain their ductility. With the increased weight fraction of particles, a drop-in ductility can be due to the void nucleation in advance and this appears clearly in the outer and transition zones. Due to the stress concentration in the AZ91 alloy at the pores of the particles and or sharp corner of the SiCp, micro plasticity took place in the FG composites.

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The Young's modulus of the film calculated from 14 micro-tensile tests was 66 ± 3 GPa, yield strength and UTS ∼1 GPa, and a total elongation of about 2%, with a ductility of ∼0.2%. The comparison to the AlCuMgMn number 2024-T8 with E = 73 GPa, yield strength of 450 MPa, and UTS of almost 500 MPa, and ductility of 6% is very interesting. The elastic moduli are in a very good agreement, but the yield strength of the AlCuMgMn micro-tensile samples do not match the value of the standard 2024 alloy: it is basically twice as high. These high values are however not unrealistic: some bulk, specially treated aluminum alloys like Al(9.5%Cu–3%Mg–1.5%Cu–0.4%Mn–0.04%Zr), AlNiMnZr, AlNiMn or Al(1.7%Fe) have very high values of yield strength and UTS between 800 and 900 MPa . The ductility of the 2024 alloy is more than twice higher than the AlCuMgMn film, which makes the thin Al alloy film rather a brittle material. Table 2 presents a comparison of mechanical properties of the investigated as-deposited AlCuMgMn thin film with some bulk Al alloys with very high values of yield strength and UTS.

Both YS and UTS increased after the TMP. Accordingly, the YS and UTS increased by ∼110% and ∼67% to ∼530 MPa and ∼880 MPa, respectively. In addition, the fracture strain of sample X1 HEA is found to be ∼38.9%, which is an increase of 24.7%. For sample X1, YS and UTS are measured at ∼360 MPa and ∼837 MPa, respectively. It is evident that the sample X2 has better UTS than the X1. The enhancement in the strength of sample X2 HEA is attributed to twinning in the deformation process and provides additional work hardening []. Work hardening plot of CoCrFeNiAl0.4 HEA is shown in Fig. 2b. The interaction of dislocations and twins leads to HEA with good mechanical properties and high work hardening ability [,].

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is ∼141 MPa for NiCoCr?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Commercial Ni-base superalloys also contain a significant amount of Cr and Co for corrosion resistance and solid solution strengthening of alloy matrix . Among them, the most typical NiCoCr-based superalloys are IN 939 and Waspaloy ,17]. There is a widely accepted assumption in the field of multi-component alloys regarding the existence of the “sluggish diffusion” effect. It indicates that the vacancy formation and migration kinetics in the multi-component alloy are slowed down by local variations in lattice potential energy caused by different types of atoms on each lattice site . This could exert an obvious effect on the creep properties in alloys at elevated temperatures instead of cryogenic temperatures, as the coarsening rate of precipitates and creep deformation is decelerated due to the low diffusion rate. However, it has been reported that the high-temperature strength of single-phase FCC HEAs is relatively insufficient due to the limitation of the solid solution strengthening effect ,22]. Therefore, the strength of NiCoCr alloy drops dramatically to a low level when the temperature exceeds 650 °C. The yield strength of NiCoCr at 700 °C is only ∼141 MPa , which is much lower than commercial metallic structural materials. For alloys with promising above-mentioned structure, it is beneficial to improve the high-temperature mechanical properties of NiCoCr alloys in order to satisfy the requirement of practical utilization, which covers a broader temperature range, similar to austenite steels and superalloys .
DOI:10.1016/j.jmst.2021.08.024

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 845 MPa for 9Cr F?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Fig. 4 shows the engineering stress–engineering strain curves for 9Cr F/M ODS steel tested at RT, 300 °C, and 600 °C. The material's yield strength (YS) at these three temperatures was 845 MPa, 665 MPa, and 216 MPa, respectively, and ultimate tensile strength (UTS) was 915 MPa (εUTS=0.061), 760 MPa (εUTS=0.058), and 268 MPa (εUTS=0.051), respectively. The total elongation dropped from 0.16 at RT to 0.117 at 300 °C, and increased to 0.245 at 600 °C, which agreed well with the results of the ODS EUROFER97 alloy reported in Ref. . Although the tensile tests were stopped periodically in order to measure responses at different axial positions at the same load, the stress–strain curves were smooth with the exception of the curve of 300 °C. The serrations in the stress–strain diagram at 300 °C were ascribed to dynamic strain aging (DSA) . The DSA in an RAFM steel has been reported at the intermediate temperature around 650 K, similar temperature to the current study . The serrations were accompanied by an increase in work-hardening and a loss of ductility.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2015.04.037

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 213 MPa for Mg-14Li-1Al?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Mg-14Li-1Al/MWCNTs composites were prepared by electrophoretic deposition and accumulative roll bonding (ARB). The mechanical properties and microstructure was characterized by tensile test, microhardness, optical microscope and transmission electron microscope. After 6 cycles of ARB, the yield strength, ultimate tensile strength and elongation of the composite are 213 MPa, 266 MPa and 21.6%, respectively. Compared with the as-cast alloy and as-rolled alloy, the yield strength and ultimate tensile strength of the composite were increased by 142.04% and 82.05%, 79.72% and 60.24%, respectively. A highest specific strength of 189 kNm/kg was obtained. The highest microhardness value of the composite is 84.6 HV, which is 66.86% and 49.73% higher than that of the as-cast (50.7 HV) and as-rolled (56.5 HV) alloys. High density dislocations, ultrafine grains, and carbon nanotubes with uniform dispersion were observed in the Mg-14Li-1Al/MWCNTs composites. The strengthening of the composites is mainly contributed to fine grain strengthening and dislocation strengthening.
DOI:10.1016/j.jmapro.2021.10.040

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The measured mechanical properties of all the sample sets are shown in Fig. 7. The control film (Set I) exhibits the lowest tensile strength of 33.7 MPa, with the highest maximum elongation-at-break of 43.6 % (Fig. 7a, b). With the addition of NCC, the tensile strength increases significantly [Set III (B and C)] and the elongation-at-break is drastically reduced. Set III (C) records the highest tensile strength of 57.2 MPa for a non- crosslinked film, with an elongation-at-break of 15.4 %. The tannic acid crosslinker also increases the tensile strength in sample Set II and IV. A steady increment was observed in sample Set II. Set II (B) recorded a tensile strength of 45.2 MPa with an elongation-at-break of 30.3 %. Set IV (D) had the highest tensile strength of 61.5 MPa. This is 82.6 % increase when compared to neat chitosan and has an elongation-at-break of 14.0 %. The addition of tannic acid appears to have no effect on the elongation of the Set IV nanocomposite films. The addition of NCC and tannic acid (Set II, III and IV) increases the Young’s Modulus (stiffness) of the chitosan matrix (Fig. 7c). The Set IV films have much greater stiffness than Set I. In fact, films in Set III (C), Set IV (C, D, E and F) are more than twice as stiff as Set I.

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of rGO/Ti?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Ti matrix composites reinforced with 0.6 wt% reduced graphene oxide (rGO) sheets were fabricated using spark plasma sintering (SPS) technology at different sintering temperatures from 800 °C to 1100 °C. Effects of SPS sintering temperature on microstructural evolution and mechanical properties of rGO/Ti composites were studied. Results showed that with an increase in the sintering temperature, the relative density and densification of the composites were improved. The Ti grains were apparently refined owing to the presence of rGO. The optimum sintering temperature was found to be 1000 °C with a duration of 5 min under a pressure of 45 MPa in vacuum, and the structure of rGO was retained. At the same time, the reaction between Ti matrix and rGO at such high sintering temperatures resulted in uniform distribution of micro/nano TiC particle inside the rGO/Ti composites. The sintered rGO/Ti composites exhibited the best mechanical properties at the sintering temperature of 1000 °C, obtaining the values of micro-hardness, ultimate tensile strength, 0.2% yield strength of 224 HV, 535 MPa and 446 MPa, respectively. These are much higher than the composites sintered at the temperature of 900 °C. The fracture mode of the composites was found to change from a predominate trans-granular mode at low sintering temperatures to a ductile fracture mode with quasi-cleavage at higher temperatures, which is consistent with the theoretical calculations.
DOI:10.1016/j.ceramint.2018.06.252

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of S0-0Ta?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The engineering tensile stress-strain curves of the stabilized and 408 h-aged alloys at room-temperature are shown in Fig. 9, from which the yield strength σYS, tensile strength σUTS, and elongation to fracture δ are measured and listed in Table 3. All these alloys after stabilization treatment exhibit high tensile strength with σUTS > 490 MPa and good ductility with δ > 30%. Among them, the highest σUTS (611 MPa) of the base S0-0Ta alloy is attributed to the relatively-small matrix grains in comparison with those of the S1-0.2Ta and S2-0.3Ta alloys, at the expense of ductility. Although the grain size of the S1-0.2Ta alloy is comparable to that of the S2 alloy, its strength (σUTS = 507 MPa) is slightly higher than that of the S2-0.3Ta (σUTS = 491 MPa), which might be due to the less amount of precipitation of Cr23C6 on GBs and fine particles in the matrix in the latter S2-0.3Ta (Fig. 2(e and f). After 408 h-aging, the strength of these aged alloys can be enhanced by the precipitation of large amount of second particles. The mechanical properties of S1-0.2Ta and S2-0.3Ta alloys are very stable even after a long-term aging, which is resulted from the high microstructural stability. The relatively higher strength (σUTS = 667 MPa) of the S0-0Ta is attributed to the precipitation of a large amount of coarse σ particles both on GBs and in the matrix. The addition of Ta inhibits the precipitation of brittle σ phase effectively, which keeps the mechanical properties (σUTS ~ 500 MPa) of aged alloys comparable to those at the stabilization stage.
DOI:10.1016/j.matchemphys.2019.122306

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 5% for TNFC-C3/PVA?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The surface and cross-sectional SEM micrographs obtained from TNFC-C3/PVA composite membranes are presented in Fig. 3. In Fig. 3a, b, it can be clearly observed that the PVA has a homogeneous and dense structure, in which TNFC-C3 phase forms small particles (~ 0.5–2 μm) due to the low addition of TNFC-C3. The rough fracture surface can reflect the ductility of 5%-TNFC-C3/PVA composite. When TNFC-C3 content reaches 50 wt%, the composite has a porous structure as evidenced in Fig. 3d. Figure 3c shows that it can be seen microscale aggregates with the increase content of TNFC-C3, and the surface of the composites becomes rough. As for 95%-TNFC-C3/PVA composite, it can be found that fiber-like network seems to overlap with each other and has a random distribution (Fig. 3e). Figure 3f shows a loose structure for 95%-TNFC-C3/PVA composite in comparison with 5%-TNFC-C3/PVA composite. The main morphological difference of these two samples lies in the amphiphilicity of cellulose. The hydrophobic sides may reduce the non-polar surface area by changing the self-assembled microstructure. Hence, the fiber-like structure of TNFC-C3 is aggregated and inclined to interconnect with each other by hydrogen bonding interactions to form network-like phase within PVA matrix, which is depending on the TNFC-C3 percentage.
DOI:10.1007/s10570-019-02404-1

问题:What is the yield strength of Ti6Al4V?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Selective laser melting (SLM) provides flexibility in creating novel metal-matrix composites (MMCs) with unique microstructures and enhanced mechanical properties over conventionally manufactured MMGs. In this study, a Zr-based metallic glass (MG) decorated Ti6Al4V (Ti64) composite with a unique hybrid nanostructure and enhanced mechanical properties and wear resistance was fabricated using SLM. The results revealed that a near-full dense and crack-free Ti-based composite was produced, with its reinforcements consisting of ultrafine β dendrites set with partially crystallized MG nanobands uniformly distributed along the boundaries of the melt pool. The addition of MG significantly affected the solidification behavior of the Ti-liquid because of its higher dynamic viscosity and density as well as compositional effect on the phase stability. With such a unique nanostructured reinforcement, the Ti64/MG composite exhibited an enhanced yield strength (>1 GPa) with reasonable ductility and fracture toughness. On the basis of the result of a theoretical analysis, we attributed the main strengthening mechanism to Orowan strengthening. The wear resistance was also much improved in the Ti64/MG composite, arising from the higher hardness of the nanostructured reinforcement and the formation of a more protective tribo-oxide layer during sliding. The confinement of the 3D distributed reinforcement phase played a crucial role in preventing the delamination of the tribo-layer on the matrix. This work opens a pathway to the design of novel additively manufactured MMCs with outstanding mechanical properties.
DOI:10.1016/j.addma.2018.12.006

问题:Which compound has a ultimate tensile strength of ∼532 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):To provide insight to the relationships between quenching rate and aging treatment on the microstructure and properties of Al–Zn–Mg alloys, 7046 A alloy, a low Cu-content commercial Al–Zn–Mg alloy, was selected in the present study. As-received hot-extruded 7046 A alloys were quenched in air and room temperature water and then aged at 120 °C for 0–120 h. The mechanical behavior, corrosion resistance and microstructures of the aged alloy were compared and analyzed. The experiment result shows that quenching rate exhibits a greater influence on the precipitates and precipitation free zones (PFZs) of aged alloys than over-aging treatment and the reasons have been discussed. The η equilibrium phases preferentially take the Al3Zr and Al6Mn dispersoids as the heterogeneous nucleation core and grow quickly inside the grains during the slow cooling process. Over-aging treatment would broaden the grain boundary PFZ and the space of grain boundary precipitates, thus improving the corrosion resistance of water quenched alloys, while it is harmful to air quenched (AQ) alloys. Pitting resistance can be summarized as: AQ24 < AQ120 < AQ60, WQ24 < WQ60 < WQ120. The yield strength, ultimate tensile strength, and elongation the T6 treated WQ alloys (∼120 °C/24 h) are ∼532 MPa, ∼568 MPa, ∼12.4%, respectively; and the corresponding values of T6 treated AQ alloys (∼120 °C/60 h) are ∼475 MPa, ∼521 MPa, ∼11.4%. Long-term over-aging (120 °C/120 h) has little effect on the hardness, while the tensile properties decrease by ∼5–6% compared to T6 treatment.
DOI:10.1016/j.jallcom.2020.157272

Results showed that intermetallic compounds were deteriorated the mechanical properties of joints, and the joint failed before the martensitic platform appeared. W13Re7 and Ti2Ni granular shaped were distributed at the shoulder affected zone (SAZ), and this region was embrittled. Removing the brittle layer of welds surface via post-welded pickling (PWP), the tensile strength of joint at a rotation speed of 600 rpm reached 751 MPa, which reached 79.1% compared with that of the base metal (BM). After the friction stir welding (FSW), the DSC was exhibited the martensite–austenite phase transformation (B19′–B2) in the pin affected zone (PAZ) and SAZ, shown shape memory effect. Phase transformation temperatures (TTs) drifted forward to positive direction about 20 K after welding. Transformation hysteresis in SAZ decreased from 19.46 °C to 12.36 °C with increasing rotation speed from 475 rpm to 950 rpm under heat and deformation of FSW process.

问题:What is the yield strength of RGO/Cu?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Ball milling is the most common and high-controllable mixing powder method. If ball milling could be utilized appropriately to control the damage to graphene and avoid the agglomeration of graphene, it would contribute to promoting the large-scale applications of graphene reinforced metal matrix composites. In this study, we propose an easy manufacturing process called “multimodal powder metallurgy” to fabricate RGO/Cu composites with heterogeneous grain structures. Different from conventional powder mixing process, the suggested strategy, which is based on high temperature oxidation and high energy ball milling, pays attention to the fabrication of the multimodal powder and the nano-level dispersion of the reinforcement. High temperature oxidation is used to produce oxidation film and reduce the ductility of electrolytic Cu powder, which contributes to synthesizing the multimodal powder by following high energy ball milling and constructing heterogeneous grain structures by consolidation. The improved powder mixing process for fabricating RGO/Cu composite powders involves the formation of the combined structure of RGO covered with Cu nanoparticles. The Cu nanoparticles, which are formed via epitaxial growth of Cu adatoms during thermal reduction, exist in the RGO layers, therefore realizing the nano-level dispersion of RGO and providing high interfacial bonding strength between RGO and Cu matrix. As a result, the RGO/Cu composites fabricated by multimodal powder metallurgy exhibit enhanced mechanical properties and high strengthening efficiency. RGO/Cu composite with 2.47 vol% RGO content had a yield strength of 197 MPa, which was 2 times higher than that of the pure Cu, and the uniform elongation was 21.8%. According to the compassion with reported researches on graphene/copper composites [,,], it could be inferred that the heterogenous grain structure is conducive to improving the strength-ductility synergy of composites.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2021.142438

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of Mg-14Li?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The typical engineering tensile stress-strain curves of as-extruded Mg-14Li and Mg-14Li-6Zn-1Y alloys are shown in Fig. 5. To describe and compare the measured data conveniently, their mechanical properties in terms of 0.2% proof yield strength (YS), ultimate tensile strength (UTS) and elongation ratio to failure (EL) are summarized in Table 1. It can be seen that tensile strength of Mg-14Li alloy along TD is obviously higher than that along ED (Fig. 5(a)). YS and UTS of “TD” samples are 106 MPa and 131 MPa, whereas the associated values of “ED” samples are 82 MPa and 111 MPa, respectively. Meanwhile, EL of “TD” samples is only half of that of “ED” samples. In general, mechanical anisotropy is a reflection of high mechanical strength and low ductility in one direction, but they are reversed in another perpendicular direction []. However, for Mg-14Li-6Zn-1Y alloy, both the tensile strength and elongation of “TD” samples are much lower than those of “ED” samples (Fig. 5(b)). YS, UTS and EL of “TD” samples are respectively 137 MPa, 156 MPa and 13.5%, whereas the counterparts of “ED” samples are respectively 149 MPa, 168 MPa and 28.2%. This anomalous phenomenon was not observed in HCP-structured and (HCP + BCC) duplex structured Mg-Li based alloys [,]. Moreover, compared with Mg-14Li alloy, Mg-14Li-6Zn-1Y alloy has higher mechanical strength.
DOI:10.1016/j.jmst.2019.07.028
