文献片段(英文原文,作证据):One benefit of the CGC is their processability, which will enable the material to be shaped for different applications. The density and mechanical properties can provide important information on whether these materials will withstand industrial conditions. The densities of the crystalline mixtures, and of the CGC samples, were measured with gas pycnometry. The densities of the CGCs were all higher than the corresponding initial crystal powder mixtures, e.g., from 1.62 ± 0.03 to 1.78 ± 0.08 g/cm3 for (MIL-53)(ZIF-62)(25/75) crystal and (MIL-53)0.25(agZIF-62)0.75 CGC, respectively (Supplementary Fig. 25). The mechanical properties of the composite glass samples were probed by nanoindentation on polished surfaces (Supplementary Figs. 26 and 27). The Young’s modulus (E) increased for both (MIL-53)0.25(agZIF-62)0.75 (E ≈ 7.7 GPa) and (UiO-66)0.25(agZIF-62)0.75 (E ≈ 7.9 GPa) compared with the pure agZIF-62 counterpart (E ≈ 5.8 GPa), which correlates well with their densities, as well as the larger (obtained through quantum chemistry calculations) elastic moduli of both MIL-53 (E = 25 GPa) and UiO-66(Zr) (E = 49 GPa). This observation suggests the composite glass has improved mechanical rigidity against irreversible plastic deformation.

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of ZnGA?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The purpose of this research was to investigate the effects of catalyst preparation temperature and time, and catalyst size in relation to the performance of the zinc glutarate (ZnGA) catalyst and the polypropylene carbonate (PPC) produced. Various ZnGA catalysts were synthesized at several combinations of temperature (40 °C, 50 °C, 60 °C and 70 °C) and time (3 h, 6 h and 9 h). The ZnGA were then used to copolymerize propylene oxide and carbon dioxide to produce PPC. It is found that the catalyst preparation temperature plays a dominant role in affecting the catalytic activity of the ZnGA. The ZnGA catalyst produced at higher temperature were found to possess higher crystallinity and capable to produce higher yield and selectivity PPC. However, the incorporation of glutaric acid into the catalyst is vital as well. Although some of the ZnGA exhibit high crystallinity, their low intensity of characteristic peaks of zinc-carboxylate bond (COO–) may causes low yield of PPC with byproducts. This is especially found on the catalysts synthesized at lower temperature (40 °C). Among the all the combinations of temperature and time, the ZnGA prepared using 9 h at 70 °C possess the highest crystallinity (42.52%) and has the best incorporation of GA (1.24 a.u.). It is capable of producing PPC with high molecular weight (97,100 Da) and narrow PDI (1.28), high crystallinity (23.44%), high and narrow Tm at 244 °C with excellent mechanical properties (tensile strength of 8.48 MPa, tensile elongation >400%, and Young’s modulus of 748.7 MPa).

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of ArH?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The TEM image is shown in Fig. 20 reveals the accumulation of dislocations around the laves phase in ArH welds and the resulting in a crack initiation around it. Generally, the laves phase acts as stress concentration sites, which attract hydrogen accumulation. It leads to a reduction of cohesive strength localized manner [,]. In addition to the laves phase, the presence of high angle SGBs in the welds can also act preferential sites for hydrogen-assisted cracking []. Oriani et al. [] notified that the hydrogen lowers the cohesive force between the atoms around trapping sites. During the tensile test, the local stress exceeded the cohesive force by the hydrogen and resulted in premature failure. Moreover, the fine-grained microstructure of ArH welds provides a larger grain boundary area, and the finer laves phase acts as potential trapping sites for hydrogen entrapment [,]. Thus, the autogenous ArH weld failed with the least elongation due to hydrogen entrapment. The severity of hydrogen in the filler metal welds are minimum, the UTS of autogenous ArH (908 MPa) is only 4% lower value than Ar weld (943 MPa). The absence of laves phases led to lesser hydrogen trapping sites and further reduced the hydrogen-assisted cracking in the filler added ArH shielded welds. SGBs and twins which evolve during plastic deformation and hinder the movement of dislocations due to the pileup of dislocations at the grain boundaries [].

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of 14H-LPSO?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The homogenized Mg-13Gd-4Y-2Zn-0.6Zr alloy was subjected to different equal channel angular pressing (ECAP) passes to investigate the effect of long period-stacking order (LPSO) phase on microstructure evolution and mechanical properties. The heterogeneous bimodal microstructure can be obtained due to the inhibition effect of intragranular 14H-LPSO phase on dislocation slip and lattice rotation at early stage. The intragranular 14H-LPSO phase was kinked and broken, which facilitate the transformation from LAGBs to HAGBs and subdivision of the deformed grains. Furthermore, the interdendritic 14H-LPSO phase was crushed and broken with further ECAP passes, which contribute to the dynamic recrystallization (DRX) process at grain boundaries via particle-stimulated nucleation (PSN) mechanism. The microstructure after 3p-ECAP demonstrates finer randomly oriented dynamic recrystallization (DRX) grains, broken 14H-LPSO phase at grain boundaries and precipitated intragranular lamellar 14H-LPSO phase. In addition, the alloy after 3p-ECAP exhibits the ultimate tensile strength of 361 MPa, tensile yield strength of 197 MPa and fracture elongation of 9.3%. The elevated temperature ultimate tensile strength after 3p-ECAP exceeds 300 MPa at test temperature of 300 °C and strain rate of 0.001/0.003 s−1, which can be ascribed to the broken 14H-LPSO phase at grain boundaries and precipitated lamellar 14H-LPSO phase within DRX grains.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2018.12.024

问题:Which compound has a yield strength of 189.6 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Fig. 9 is the tensile engineering stress–strain curves and mechanical properties of AZ31 with different ARB temperature. Yield strength increases and the elongation of the alloy decrease significantly after ARB, as shown in Fig. 9(a). With the increase of ARB temperature, both the yield strength, ultimate tensile strength and the elongation rate also increased slightly. Therefore, it can be seen that ARB process mainly improve the yield strength of AZ31 alloy and ductility loss is severe. But as the ARB temperature increases, the yield strength, ultimate tensile strength and elongation of AZ31 alloy are slightly increased. Fig. 9(b) shows the variation of yield strength, ultimate tensile strength, and elongation of AZ31 alloy with different ARB temperature. The as sample with yield strength of 189.6 MPa, tensile strength of 271.2 MPa and elongation of 17.9%. When ARB at 400 °C, yield strength increased to 214.9 MPa and increased by 13.3%, the ultimate tensile strength decreased to 268.8 MPa, but the elongation decreased to 5.5% and decreased by 69.3%. When ARB at 450 °C, the yield strength and ultimate tensile strength of the material were increased to 222.8 MPa and 295.4 MPa, compared with the as-received sample increased by 17.5% and 8.9%, respectively, while the elongation decreased to 6.0% and decrease by 66.5%. The increase in strength of the alloy is related to strain hardening. The large decrease in plasticity is related to internal cracks. A slight improvement of plasticity can be attributed to the initiation and propagation of crack was restrained by shear band after increasing ARB temperature. The shear band is composed of fine recrystallized grains, which inhibits crack initiation and propagation. The higher the temperature, the wider the shear band.

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 801.8 MPa for Fe0.5Co0.5NiMnGa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The mechanical property is important for alloy applications. The restriction for Ni2MnGa alloy is the intrinsic brittleness. Element substitution is selected to improve the mechanical property. The compressive engineering stress - strain curves for the FexCo1-xNiMnGa alloys are plotted in Fig. 7. It is obvious that the FeNiMnGa alloy shows the largest plastic deformation, which is about 6.74%. Its yield strength and ultimate strength are 663.5 MPa and 1086.4 MPa, respectively. For the Fe0.5Co0.5NiMnGa alloy, the yield strength can increase to 801.8 MPa, while the ultimate strength decreases to 843.0 MPa. Its deformation can only be 1.68%. What is more, when Fe is completely replaced by Co, both the deformation and strength are reduced. The deformation is only 0.87%, and the yield strength is merely 567.2 MPa. This phenomenon implies that the Fe addition can improve the plasticity, while the Co addition can deteriorate it. To deeply understand the deformation mechanism, the SEM picture of the fracture surface is present in Fig. 7. All the alloys show main intergranular fracture and small cracks can be found inside fracture surface. Besides, the cleavage fracture can also be seen in the grains. Since there is no precipitates and elemental segregation along the grain boundaries, the main cause of the intergranular fracture is the weak bonding force between the grain boundaries or the large interior stress. But the smaller grain size of FeNiMnGa makes the alloy show larger plasticity which originates from the effect of grain refining.
DOI:10.1016/j.intermet.2018.05.014

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of β-STA?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In this work, the first successful attempt of additive manufacturing (AM) Ti-55531 near-β alloy fabricated by laser melting deposition (LMD) is reported. Optimized processing parameters of LMD were used to manufacture high relative density (99.8%) Ti-55531 alloy. It was found that the microstructure of as-deposited sample is mainly composed of β phase with heterogeneous distribution of α phase. The heterogeneity of microstructure of as-deposited sample was caused by the ubiquitous temperature gradient during LMD process. Thus, the as-deposited samples have a significant anisotropic tensile property which may not meet the aeronautical criterion. To overcome the issue of inhomogeneous microstructure and minimize the anisotropy, the microstructure was tailored by two kinds of heat treatment, i.e., β solution treatment subsequent aging (β-STA) and triplex annealing (α/β-STA). A transformed β microstructure was obtained in β-SAT condition, which resulted in the high ultimate tensile strength (UTS) (>1350 MPa) and low ductility (<6.5%). Besides, the bimodal structure was formed in α/β-STA condition and the associated sample shows an excellent balance of strength (UTS>1200 MPa) and ductility (>8%). Furthermore, the formation mechanism of microstructure and the subsequent impact on mechanical properties was systematically discussed. This work demonstrates the potential of fabricating Ti-55531 alloy parts by LMD and paves the way for enhancing LMD processed near β Ti-alloys through heat treatment.
DOI:10.1016/j.jallcom.2019.151792

问题:What is the ductility of Mg-0.5Zn-0.5Ca?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):A magnesium alloy containing essential, non-toxic, biodegradable elements such as Ca and Zn has been fabricated using a novel twin-roll casting process (TRC). Microstructure, mechanical properties, in vivo corrosion and biocompatibility have been assessed and compared to the properties of the rare earth (RE) element containing WE43 alloy. TRC Mg-0.5 wt% Zn- 0.5 wt% Ca exhibited fine grains with an average grain size ranging from 70 to 150 μm. Mechanical properties of a TRC Mg-0.5Zn-0.5Ca alloy showed an ultimate tensile strength of 220 MPa and ductility of 9.3%. The TRC Mg-0.5Zn-0.5Ca alloy showed a degradation rate of 0.51 ± 0.07 mm/y similar to that of the WE43 alloy (0.47 ± 0.09 mm/y) in the rat model after 1 week of implantation. By week 4 the biodegradation rates of both alloys studied were lowered and stabilized with fewer gas pockets around the implant. The histological analysis shows that both WE43 and TRC Mg-0.5Zn-0.5Ca alloy triggered comparable tissue healing responses at respective times of implantation. The presence of more organized scarring tissue around the TRC Mg-0.5Zn-0.5Ca alloys suggests that the biodegradation of the RE-free alloy may be more conducive to the tissue proliferation and remodelling process.
DOI:10.1016/j.bioactmat.2021.10.026

The total content of intermetallic particles in them is approximately the same; however, the T1 phase content in BM is higher by ~2 wt % and the δ' phase content is lower by ~2 wt %. It is important that the T1 phase is a more effective hardener as compared to the δ' phase and that its content is higher by several times. As was shown in , the yield strength of the V-1469 alloy after the optimum hardening treatment is higher than that of the grade 1441 alloy by ~200 MPa. Here, the T1 phase content in the V-1469 alloy is higher by 5–6 wt %, which determines the stronger hardening effect of the T1 phase as compared to the δ' phase (10 wt %). Thus, quantitative XRD analysis makes it possible to explain the effect of decreasing the strength properties in the welded joint and to find a way to weaken this effect by choosing heat-treatment conditions in order to increase the fraction of the T1 phase. XRD analysis will favor the optimization of searching works in this field.

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 206 GPa for steel Q345B?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Using the non-linear finite element software ABAQUS/Standard v6.10-1 , the finite element (FE) analysis was carried out. A shell element S4R from the ABAQUS library was used to model unstiffened and internally ring-stiffened DT-joints. The mesh size of the joints analyzed in Sections 3, 4 and 5 was carefully determined by a convergence study. It was found that the mesh size of 20 × 20 mm (length by width) for all joints could produce accurate joint strength predictions with reasonably computational cost. Both geometric and material nonlinearities were considered. The nonlinear geometric parameter (*NLGEOM) in ABAQUS was used to consider the effect of large displacement. The yield stress (fy), elastic modulus (E) and the Poisson's ratio (v) of steel Q345B which is commonly used in Chinese construction industry are 345 N/mm2, 206GPa, and 0.3 respectively. The material curve used was an elastic-perfectly plastic curve. In ABAQUS simulation, Von-Misses yield criterion was adopted. The weld was not included in the modeling due to its insignificant effect on the stiffened joint strength .
DOI:10.1016/j.jcsr.2016.06.012

Compared to the Mg-9.8Gd-2.7Y-0.4Zr alloy, the Ag-containing Mg-8.5Gd-2.3Y-1.8Ag-0.4Zr alloy exhibited superior tensile properties in all conditions.

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Fig. 10a shows the representative tensile curves of the AE432 alloy at 20 °C, 150 °C, 200 °C, 250 °C, and 300 °C. The results indicate that this alloy exhibits high yield strength and significant strain hardening behavior at room temperature, but both the yield strength and the strain hardening decrease with the increase of testing temperature. From the tensile curves, the values of the tensile properties for this alloy, including the ultimate tensile strength (UTS), the tensile yield strength (TYS), and the elongation to failure (ε), are plotted in Fig. 10b. It is obvious that both UTS and TYS decrease as testing temperature rises while ε goes down after rising first and reaches the maximum at 150 °C. The UTS, TYS, and ε of this alloy are approximately 266 MPa, 170 MPa, and 11.2%, respectively, at room temperature, which are greater by approximately 9%, 17%, and 7%, respectively, than those of the conventional AE44 alloy . In addition, the AE432 alloy also exhibits better high temperature tensile properties than the conventional AE44 alloy. For instance, the UTS and the TYS of AE432 alloy are 134 and 109 MPa, respectively, at 200 °C while those of the conventional AE44 alloy are approximately 120 and 100 MPa , respectively, although both alloys have a comparative ε of approximately 24%. Finally, the studied alloy also shows better tensile properties than those of the other conventional heat-resistant as-cast magnesium alloys, such as AS41, AS21, AE42, AX51, AX52, AJ52, AJ62, AXJ531, and so on .
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2015.01.050

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Hot stamping is an established process for the manufacturing of high strength components for lightweight construction . Within the direct hot stamping process (Fig. 49b) an aluminum–silicon coated blank is heated up above the Ac3-temperature of the material and dwelled for a certain time to ensure a homogeneous austenitic microstructure. Afterwards, the blank is transferred to a press in which it is formed and simultaneously quenched by tool contact (Fig. 49) . With cooling rates above 27 K/s the commonly used boron-manganese steel 22MnB5 develops a martensitic microstructure with an ultimate tensile strength of 1500 MPa and an ultimate elongation of 5–6% .

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 10% for poly ( lactic acid )?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):This work is aimed to study the suitability of a bio based compound, cardanol acetate (CA), as plasticizing agent of poly(lactic acid) (PLA). Compared to other natural derived plasticizers, cardanol acetate is not obtained from food crops but as a by product of cashew nut extraction. In addition, the cardanol derived plasticizers can be obtained by the use of non toxic and low environmental impact reagents. The plasticizing effectiveness of cardanol acetate was confirmed by the decrease of the glass transition temperature and flexural modulus, which were comparable to those obtained by the use of conventional oil based plasticizers, such as diethylhexyl phthalate (DEHP). In addition, calorimetric analysis revealed that the addition of the plasticizer, both cardanol derived and phthalate, involves a significant increase of the crystallization kinetics. An analysis of the flexural strength and deformation at break indicated that the increase of the crystallization kinetics has more dramatic effects compared to the decrease of the glass transition, particularly at low plasticizer content, finally leading to a decrease of the ductility. At higher plasticizer content, an increase of the ductility is observed, and PLA plasticized by 10% of CA showed a significant higher deformation at break than PLA plasticized by DEHP. In addition, plasticizer migration tests showed a lower weight loss of PLA plasticized by CA compared to DEHP plasticized PLA, which indicates the potential higher stability of properties of the cardanol derived plasticizer.
DOI:10.1016/j.polymdegradstab.2016.02.020

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In this study, we prepared nickel and phosphorus decorated graphene nanoflakes (Ni–P@GNFs) as a reinforcement in Ti–6Al–4V (TC4) alloy matrix by an electroless plating method. It can enhance the interface bonding force between GNFs and TC4 matrix, and facilitate uniform dispersion of the GNFs in the TC4 matrix. This in turn relieve severe interface reactions between the metal and the carbon nanomaterial. The Ni–P@GNFs/TC4 composites with different GNFs content (0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1, 1.5 wt.%) were fabricated via short-term ball milling and spark plasma sintering (SPS). The effects of reinforcement content on microstructures and mechanical properties of Ni–P@GNFs/TC4 composites were studied while the sintered composites were characterized by X-Ray diffraction (XRD), Raman spectroscopy, scanning electron microscopy (SEM), and transmission electron microscopy (TEM). The results indicated that the mechanical properties of Ni–P@GNFs/TC4 composites were dramatically improved due to the homogeneous dispersion of GNFs in the TC4 matrix. The compressive strength of 0.5 wt.% Ni–P@GNFs/TC4 composite reaches 1133 MPa: this was an increase of 30.6% versus TC4 (867 MPa) while maintaining 34.2% ductility. The strengthening mechanism of the mechanical properties of the composites are discussed.
DOI:10.1016/j.jmrt.2021.11.109

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 1.51 ± 0.29 MPa for citral?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Incorporation of EOs significantly influenced the mechanical properties of SS/GG films (Table 2). The TS of the control (2.35 ± 0.29 MPa) film was significantly reduced with the introduction of EO into SS/GG matrix. The lowest value was recorded (1.39 ± 0.20 MPa) for the film impregnated with citral, and a slight improvement in TS (1.51 ± 0.29 MPa) occurred when both EOs were added. The significant decrease in the TS of the EO-based biopolymer films could be influenced by the plasticization or lubrication effect attributed to the EOs. Similar drops in the TS with the addition of EOs were reported for edible films prepared with kafirin/quercetin/citral, chitosan/carvacrol/grape seed extract, apple puree/pectin/carvacrol and tomato puree/pectin/carvacrol (Du et al., 2008; Giteru et al., 2015; Rubilar et al., 2013; Du et al., 2008). In addition, edible films formulated with water chestnut starch and chitosan also showed a similar trend in the TS, where addition of combination of antimicrobial compounds such as Cornus officinalis fruit extract/glycerol monolaurate, glycerol monolaurate/nisin and glycerol monolaurate/pine needle essential oil reduced the initial TS of the polymer itself (Mei et al., 2013). Plasticizing effect of the essential oils can be attributed to the reduction in the tensile strength of EOs added SS/GG films. The addition of EO reduces the interactions between polymer-polymer chains and provides a flexible domain within the film matrix resulting in a decrease in TS and increase in EAB (Ahmed et al., 2016). On the other hand, the addition of EOs can create heterogeneous thin film structures with discontinuities in the film, and EO droplets impregnated in the film can be deformed at room temperature, thereby decreasing the TS values (Fabra, Talens, & Chiralt, 2008).
DOI:10.1016/j.fpsl.2019.100380

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of Y2O3?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Figure 11 shows machined tensile specimens for the LC, RC, 2TB, 2TB–0.5YO, 2TB–1YO, and 2TB–1.5YO hybrid composites, from top to bottom. Figure 12 shows the stress–strain diagram of the developed samples. The LC sample processed at the temperature of 720 °C was set as a reference for comparison with the composites. The results show that the LC, RC, and 2TB samples have exhibited a UTS of 171 MPa, 195 MPa, and 285 MPa, respectively. 12% and 40% increments in UTS were achieved for the RC and 2TB composite samples, respectively, compared to that of the LC. The UTS increased substantially with an increment in the proportion of the Y2O3 particles. 42%, 44%, and 45% increments in UTS were achieved for the 2TB–0.5YO, 2TB–1YO, and 2TB–1.5YO hybrid composites, respectively, compared to that of the LC. Small additions of hard ceramic particles into the matrix contribute to a substantial improvement in mechanical properties such as hardness and strength, compared to the unreinforced one . The significant increase in UTS of the developed composites is due to the uniform distribution of the TiB2 and Y2O3 particles in the matrix (Fig. 5b–e). As the amount of the Y2O3 reinforcement increased, the particles somewhat got agglomerated. The clustering of reinforcement particles causes the properties of the composite to deteriorate. It hinders the distribution of the particulates in the matrix, which in turn increases the porosity level and voids in the developed composites . The increase in the UTS achieved was due to the strong bond between the reinforcement and the matrix. Due to this, the load transfer is more efficient for the stir rheocast composites. Moreover, the grains were refined with an increase in the proportion of Y2O3 particles and improved the UTS of the developed composites.

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 80 GPa for Ti-Zr-Mo-Sn-Nb?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In our previous work, a series of low-E Ti-Zr-Mo-Sn-Nb alloys with E = 40 to 80 GPa were designed based on the cluster-plus-glue-atom model for stable solid solutions. In this model, the atoms are ideally arranged in accordance with their atomic interactions, where the strongly bonded atoms form the nearest-neighbor coordination polyhedral clusters, and those weakly bonded are glue atoms located outside the clusters, being expressed with the cluster formula [cluster](glue atoms). In the investigated β-Ti alloy system, Mo and Sn show negative enthalpies of mixing, ΔH, with the base solvent Ti and its similar element Zr. Nb has positive ΔH values with Ti and Zr. So, the ideal structure can be formulated as [(Mo,Sn)-(Ti,Zr)14]-Nbx, where the square-bracketed part is the CN14 cluster (CN: coordination number) centered by Mo and/or Sn to represent the local short-range order (SRO) in the BCC lattice, and then these clusters are glued by Nb atoms. In order to reach the appropriate β-Ti stability with a sufficient-low E, the β-stabilizing elements (Mo, Nb) should be balanced with the low-E elements (Zr, Sn). Here, this kind of cluster formula can provide a framework to adjust these four alloying solute elements, Mo, Nb, Zr, and Sn in the base Ti.

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):A hypereutectic Nb60Ti24Ni16 (at%) alloy was prepared by vacuum induction melting, and a nano lamellae NbTi–NiTi composite was obtained by hot-forging and wire-drawing of the ingot. Microscopic analysis showed that NbTi and NiTi nano lamellae distributed alternatively in the composite, and aligned along the wire axial direction, with a high volume fraction (~70%) of NbTi nano lamellae. In situ synchrotron X-ray diffraction analysis revealed that stress induced martensitic transformation occurred upon loading, which would effectively weaken the stress concentration at the interface and avoid the introduction of defects into the nano reinforced phase. Then the embedded NbTi nano lamellae exhibited a high elastic strain up to 2.72%, 1.5 times as high as that of the Nb nanowires embedded in a conventional plastic matrix, and the corresponding stress carried by NbTi was evaluated as 2.53 GPa. The high volume fraction of NbTi nano lamellae improved the translation of high strength from the nano reinforced phase into bulk properties of the composite, with a platform stress of ~1.7 GPa and a fracture strength of ~1.9 GPa.

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):To overcome this problem, in the present work, different wt% of MC Al powders were added with the NC Al-GNP powder mixtures. The bimodal mixtures were sintered by SPS and subsequently the consolidated samples were hot rolled. Fig. 9c–e shows the variations in YS, UTS, and elongation of the nanocomposites (before and after rolling) with respect to MC Al weight percentage. It is clear that as the amount of MC Al was increased, the YS and UTS decreased, whereas, the elongation increased. Hot rolled samples showed a marginal increase in the ductility in comparison to the sintered samples, as shown in the stress vs strain graphs (Fig. 9b), which is due to improved densification after hot rolling. The second possibility of enhanced ductility of the rolled samples might be due to the recrystallization in which a new set of strain free and equiaxed grains would have been formed []. The recrystallization process depends on both the temperature and time, however, in the present study the possibility of recrystallization in the rolled samples is minimal owing to the low processing temperature (150 °C). The good combination of strength and ductility was observed in the rolled Al30M-70N-GNP nanocomposite as it exhibited better elongation (11.4%) while maintaining substantial yield strength (169 MPa) in comparison to rolled Al100N-GNP (elongation: 5.6%, YS: 186 MPa).

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):When the x value increases to 1.1, the Fe2Ni2CrAl1.1 alloy has a yield strength of 973.9 MPa, a fracture strength of 2894 MPa, and plastic strain of 48.7%, respectively. It can be deduced that increasing volume fraction of the BCC phase contributes to the enhancement of strength. For alloys with relatively high Al concentration (x>1.2), a further increase from 2894 MPa to 2961 MPa can be observed in the strength of Fe2Ni2CrAl1.2 alloy, with the plastic strain decreasing to 42.3%. With further increase in concentration of Al, the strength of the Fe2Ni2CrAl1.3 alloy decreases to 2811 MPa, accompanied by a reduced plastic strain of 36.9%. From the above analysis, the compressive fracture strength of the as-cast Fe2Ni2CrAl1.2 alloy is the highest among the developed alloys. Tailoring the Al concentration in the Fe2Ni2CrAlx alloy system can tune the mechanical properties of the alloys. The alloy with high FCC phase content exhibits relatively low strength but extended ductility. Enhanced strengthening can be derived via the increasing Al concentration, accompanied by only a small loss of plasticity. Accordingly, the addition of Al contributes to the presence of a desirable microstructure and enhancement in mechanical properties, which was quite attractive for the MPEAs in the as-cast condition.

问题:What is the yield strength of NiCoFe?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The outstanding strength-ductility combination has always been a challenging problem in the development of metal materials, high entropy alloys/medium entropy alloys (HEAs/MEAs) are considered to be the key material to solve this problem. Here a novel non-equiatomic Ni46Co24Fe24Al3Ti3 MEA with equiaxed grains was prepared by vacuum suspension melting, which consists of FCC matrix and heterogeneous L12 precipitate. In the current work, the different states of the Ni46Co24Fe24Al3Ti3 MEA from as-cast to after thermo-mechanical processing (TMP) were systematically researched. The result shows that the heterogeneous L12 precipitate of the as-cast alloy is composed of cubic precipitates at the grain boundary and spherical precipitates in the grain interior. After TMP, a short-rod L12 precipitated by discontinuous precipitation reaction (DP) at the grain boundary, and a spherical L12 precipitated by continuous precipitation reaction (CP) in the grain interior. Meanwhile, the alloy shows outstanding mechanical properties in the tensile test at room temperature. The ultimate tensile strength of the as-cast alloy is 935 MPa, with an elongation of 32%. The strength of the alloy is further improved after TMP, the yield strength and ultimate tensile strength are respectively 820 MPa and 1236 MPa, with an elongation of 23% retained. Compared with the single-phase NiCoFe MEA, the yield strength and ultimate tensile strength are significantly increased by 310% and 147%. The excellent mechanical properties of the alloy are attributed to the combination of the soft FCC matrix and the strengthening of the L12 precipitate.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2022.142617

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Niobium (Nb) and molybdenum (Mo) are conventionally added to stainless steels to improve their mechanical and corrosion properties. However, the effects of Nb and Mo addition on the processing and properties in laser-powder bed fusion (L-PBF) have not been well investigated, especially in the context of 420 stainless steel. In this study, 420 stainless steel pre-alloyed with Nb (1.2 wt.%) and Mo (0.57 wt.%) was processed by L-PBF and characterized in terms of its physical, mechanical and corrosion properties as well as microstructure. The addition of Nb and Mo did not significantly affect the densification of 420 stainless steel when printed over an energy range of 28–75 J/mm3 and a maximum density of 99.3 ± 0.02% theoretical at 63 J/mm3 was achieved. In mechanical tests, L-PBF 420 stainless steel specimens exhibited higher mechanical properties in the presence of Nb and Mo. After heat treatment, the UTS of 420 stainless steel with Nb and Mo improved to 1750 ± 30 MPa and elongation to 9.0 ± 0.2%, much higher than previously reported properties achieved in L-PBF and exceeding wrought 420 stainless steel. The tempering of martensite phases as well as the presence of nanoscale NbC were found to correlate with improved mechanical properties. In electrochemical tests, 420 stainless steel exhibited slightly better corrosion properties with the addition of Nb and Mo.
DOI:10.1016/j.addma.2019.06.016

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Fig. 12 shows the stress amplitude versus the number of cycles to failure for the blasted and non-blasted specimens in the as-processed condition and after the thermal treatment at 500 °C. The effect of blasting on the fatigue strength of Ti6Al4V is noticeable . Fatigue strength of non-blasted specimens (~650 MPa) represents about 70% of the yield strength (911 MPa). Blasting with zirconia particles, Fig. 12a, increases the fatigue strength (~710 MPa) by about 10%, whereas blasting with alumina particles, Fig. 12b, causes a significant decrease (~485 MPa), which corresponds to about 50% of the yield strength. Thermal oxidation at 500 °C promotes a decrease in the fatigue strength in both cases, leading to about 450 MPa and 300 MPa for the samples blasted with zirconia and alumina, respectively. Experiments with samples treated at 700 °C were very limited because many of the samples experienced a large eccentricity. Nevertheless, experiments with sound specimens revealed a drastic decrease in the number of cycles to failure. For instance, BL–AlO samples supported only 130,000 cycles under a stress amplitude of 300 MPa.

问题:Which compound has a ultimate tensile strength of 170 GPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In the initial supply condition, S04 steel has excellent low-temperature mechanical properties. The ultimate tensile strength (UTS), yield strength (YS) and elastic modulus at − 180 °C are 1814, 1220 MPa and 170 GPa, respectively. After RHSL at − 180 °C with a stress of 1200 MPa (YS-20 MPa) for ten cycles, the mean yield strength value significantly rises to 1420 MPa (by 16.4 pct) and the elastic modulus to 205 GPa (by 20.6 pct). Increasing the stress level to 1270 MPa (YS+50 MPa) produces more significant rises in yield strength to 1650 MPa (by 35.2 pct) and in elastic modulus to 207.5 GPa. However, RHSL (for both YS-20 MPa and YS+50 MPa) only leads to a slight change in the percentage reduction of the area (see also Table I). Therefore, RHSL can produce a significant strengthening effect with little deterioration in plasticity. This implies that RHSL may become an effective and useful technology to strengthen steels and nonferrous alloys. Furthermore, it is interesting to note that RHSL has almost no/little influence on the ultimate tensile strength. After ten cycles of RHSL, the total residual strain (< 2.0 pct as seen in Table II) indicates a small plastic deformation has occurred, which also means that the steel has been strain-hardened. Therefore, after RHSL the specimens exhibit a higher YS than in the initial supply condition. However, because the steel has been strain-hardened, its ability to further strain-harden during the succeeding tension deformation in the tensile test was weakened, and this phenomenon is more enhanced in the case of YS+50 MPa (Figure 1). The strain-hardening from the succeeding tension deformation is decreased. Thus, even the YS after RHSL is increased, and the UTS is not increased, being almost the same as that in the initial supply condition.
DOI:10.1007/s11661-019-05535-z

问题:Which compound has a ultimate tensile strength of 688 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Fig. 8 describes the temperature and microstructure dependence of tensile properties of Ti-43Al-6Nb-1B alloy. And the detailed results of microstructure characteristics and tensile properties of this alloy are listed in Table 1. Fig. 8a shows the tensile strength (UTS) and Fig. 8b displays the tensile elongation (δ). As shown, the UTS of current alloy is strongly microstructure dependent; at room temperature (RT), the UTS is 670 MPa in the as-cast state, while in the as-forged state it is up to 975 MPa. The as-forged alloy maintains much high UTS not less than 950 MPa until 750 °C, while for the as-cast state the UTS is only 645 MPa at 750 °C. Both the FL and DP materials exhibit excellent UTS more than 830 MPa and 770 MPa respectively until 750 °C, which are much higher than that of the as-cast state, but lower than that of the as-forged. With temperature increasing higher than 750 °C, the UTS values of all microstructures drop sharply. At 800 °C, the UTS value drops to 610 MPa, 688 MPa, 755 MPa and 790 MPa respectively for the as-cast, DP, FL and as-forged materials, as listed in Table 1. According to the above microstructure investigation and tensile properties, three factors can account for the superior properties of this alloy after canned forging. The first, also the main factor is low Al and low Nb content in this TNB alloy, which is beneficial to increasing the ratio of α2 phase and reducing the lamellar spacing. Second, the refinement and homogenization of microstructure is obtained by forging, as well as the breakdown of TiB particles. The third is the refinement of lamellar spacing after forging. It is generally accepted that increasing the ratio of α2 phase and reducing the lamellar spacing are favorable to strengthening TiAl alloys .
DOI:10.1016/j.intermet.2012.07.016

问题:Which compound has a fracture strength of 1000 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The true fracture stresses (σf) of neutron irradiated RAFM steels is another useful quantity to compare the performance of different materials. The σf was calculated by dividing the load at fracture by the fracture surface cross-section area measured using an SEM. A comparison of σf and σPIS of the steels before and after irradiation is plotted in Fig. 11a and the σf /σPIS ratios are plotted in Fig. 11b. No significant effect of irradiation on σf and σPIS was noticeable. This is consistent with literature where it is known that: (i) σPIS of a material is unaffected by irradiation [,] and (ii) σf of 9%Cr FM steels for Tirr ≤ 300 °C and as low as 80 °C is not much modified by low neutron doses, until significant embrittlement starts for doses ≥ 10 dpa . Material K had the lowest σf at values lower than 1000 MPa. The σf of all the other steels ranged between ∼1000 – 1500 MPa, which agrees well with low dose (<10 dpa) neutron irradiated data on other bcc steels such as 9Cr-2WVTa, 9Cr-1MoVNb and commercial nuclear alloys such as A533B . The σf of most steels was larger than σPIS, including the σf /σPIS ratios being mostly >1 (Fig. 11). In agreement with similar results in literature , this is another direct evidence that most of the total plasticity occurs during necking in neutron irradiated RAFM steels. Consequentially, for a better understanding of the irradiation hardening-embrittlement behavior, the necking deformation in these materials should be given increased attention, including analysing the reduction in area (RA).
DOI:10.1016/j.fusengdes.2021.112935

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of Sn1.5Sb0.7Cu?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The results of the microhardness tests of the Sn1.5Sb0.7Cu composite solders are shown in Table 6. A comparison of microhardness results is shown in Fig. 5. It can be seen that the microhardness of the rapid-cooled Sn1.5Sb0.7Cu sample was 11.5 Hv higher than that of the slow-cooled Sn1.5Sb0.7Cu sample. The same phenomenon was also observed in the Sn1.5Sb0.7Cu composite solder (12.0 Hv of slow-cooled VS. 13.3 Hv of rapid-cooled). In addition, the average microhardness value of Sn1.5Sb0.7Cu composite solder under the slow- and rapid-cooled conditions were 14.3 to 15.7 % higher than that of undoped Sn1.5Sb0.7Cu solder, respectively. The apparent strengthening effect can be attributed to the refined β-Sn and Cu6Sn5. The typical tensile stress–strain curves of the Sn1.5Sb0.7Cu composite solders under the different cooled conditions are shown in Fig. 6. The tensile tests were conducted on four different samples taken from the same sample, and the average values of the ultimate tensile strength (UTS), 0.2 % yield strength (0.2YS), and percentage of elongation are given in Table 7. The results show that the average UTS values were 45.3 ± 2.3 and 50.2 ± 2.3 MPa, average 0.2YS values were 40.6 ± 1.9 and 44.3 ± 2.8 MPa, and average elongation percentages were 35.7 ± 2.9 and 37.2 ± 2.4 % in the slow- and rapid-cooled Sn1.5Sb0.7Cu solders, respectively. The mechanical properties of rapid-cooled Sn1.5Sb0.7Cu solder were superior to those of slow-cooled Sn1.5Sb0.7Cu solder. These values illustrate that, under the same cooling conditions, the UTS and 0.2YS of Sn1.5Sb0.7Cu composite solders have significantly higher strength than those of the Sn1.5Sb0.7Cu solders.
DOI:10.1007/s10854-015-2860-3

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of Ti-33Nb-4Sn?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Figure 3 gives the representative tensile stress-strain curves of ST and CRA Ti-33Nb-4Sn specimens. The ST specimen exhibits notable “double yielding” behavior. Obviously, the first yielding at ~107 MPa can be attributed to the stress-induced β→α′′ martensitic transformation and the reorientation of the martensite variants; the second yielding is associated with the initiation of plastic deformation. Apparently, the alloy in such a condition is not a good candidate as implant material because of its relatively low yield (107 MPa) and ultimate tensile strength (509 MPa), although it does exhibit low Young’s modulus. However, with CRA treatment, the alloy is significantly strengthened, with a yield strength in the order of ~763 MPa and ultimate tensile strength of ~853 MPa. On top of this impressive strength level, we should note that the CRA specimen also exhibits an ultralow value of Young’s modulus of ~36 GPa. To the best of our knowledge, this level of Young’s modulus is the lowest ever recorded for a solid and bulk titanium alloy and is much lower than those of the currently available biomedical Ti alloys. What is more, it is only ~6 GPa higher than that of human bone.
DOI:10.1038/srep14688

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is ∼915 MPa for 9Cr?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Fig. 2 shows the stress–strain diagram of the 9Cr ODS steel. The engineering stress, σe, was calculated by σe = F/A, where F is the load on the sample and A is the area of the cross-section. F was measured by the MTS loading frame, while A was measured before the tensile test. The applied stress on the specimen was approximated by the true stress, σt, which was calculated by engineering stress (σe) and strain (ε) by: σt = σe(1 + ε). Young’s modulus for the 9Cr ODS sample was measured to be ∼203 GPa using ultrasonic techniques. The 0.2% yield strength was measured to be ∼845 MPa. The ultimate tensile strength (UTS) was measured to be ∼915 MPa, and developed at the strain of ∼8.6%. Sample necking began after the sample reached the UTS, and continued until sample failure. The total elongation of the 9Cr ODS sample was ∼16.2%.
DOI:10.1016/j.jnucmat.2014.06.060

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Despite its many advantages including biodegradability, biocompatibility, antibacterial activity, and non-toxicity, the weak physical properties of Cs limit its wide range applications. Thus, developing Cs-based composite materials with enhanced mechanical properties while maintaining the unique properties of Cs mentioned above has been an attractive research topic in different applications . In this context, tensile test was utilized to evaluate the mechanical properties of prepared composite films by comparing with their pristine segments in terms of tensile strength, elongation at break, and Young’s modulus (Fig. 6 and Table 1). Based on the achievements from tensile test, the higher tensile strength was determined for pristine Cs with the value of 88 MPa by elongation of 9%, while the lowest determined for PP-Cl with the value of 12 MPa at 365% elongation. Furthermore, Cs gave the higher Young's modulus (1830 MPa) compared to that of PP-Cl (170 MPa). In the case of composite films, the linear relationship of mechanical features investigated with increasing Cs content is also observed, as listed in Table 1. Among the PP-Cs composite films, PP-Cs (1:2) indicated improved tensile strength but lower percent elongation due to brittle nature of Cs. These results revealed that incorporation of higher amount of PP-Cl highly increased the elasticity of the PP-Cs composite films.
DOI:10.1007/s00289-021-04064-3

问题:What is the ductility of cantilever beam?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Table 1 summarizes the optimized material volume fraction and ultimate performance of each cantilever beam shown in Fig. 14. One can see that the purely plastic design requires the least amount of material with volume fraction of 0.4302 to dissipate the prescribed amount of plastic work. If the maximum damage is constrained to be smaller than 0.2, more material (Vf = 0.4351) is needed to satisfy the damage constraint. However, with only 1.14% more material, the damage constrained design is able to achieve 23.97% increase in maximum load capacity and 65.40% increase ductility. Here, ductility is defined as the displacement at the maximum load point. More importantly, 3646.5 J plastic work can be dissipated before the complete failure, which is 99.32% more than the plastic design (1829.5 J). This trade-off is more prominent if more restricted damage constraint is considered, as indicated by the fourth and fifth row in Table 1. It can be observed that the minimum compliance design performs worst in terms of all indexes. This is expected since it is designed for minimum compliance instead of energy dissipation and damage mitigation.
DOI:10.1007/s00158-018-1984-5

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The displacement and stress field are obtained assuming an elastic thickness of 100 km, consistent with tectonics around Tharsis, and a crustal density and thickness of 2,800 kg m−3 and 50 km to satisfy InSight seismic measurements (Supplementary Table 1). Loading-induced stresses are calculated in map view at 20 km depth, which is the shallow end of the range of depths of low frequency marsquake hypocenter locations observed by InSight, and are limited by the crustal yield strength at that same depth (~140 and 440 MPa in extension and compression, respectively). The later compressional stresses from global Hesperian–Amazonian cooling (tectonic stress of 105 to 215 MPa, based on strains of 1 to 2 × 10−3) are added and are also limited by the crustal yield strength.

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 611 MPa for 316L?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):To distinguish the influence of the WJP treatment on the properties and surface characteristics of the weld, Fig. 18 presents the engineering stress–strain curve on the surface of the small specimen for the parent specimen, as-welded specimen, and WJP treated specimen. The yield and tensile strengths of the 316L parent material were 290 and 738 MPa, respectively. The specimen dimension only had a slight influence on mechanical performance. Both the yield and tensile strengths decreased for the small specimen due to the effects of constraints. The yield strength of the untreated welded joint was 310 MPa, whereas its tensile strength was 611 MPa. After the WJP treatment, these strengths grew by 16.1% (360 MPa) and 23.3% (674 MPa), respectively. The WJP treatment had a greater effect on the mechanical performance of the surface material than on that of the entire joint. The increase in the microhardness of the surface and subsurface as induced by WJP treatment can improve the material resistance of the weld to plastic deformation, thus improving its tensile mechanical properties. In addition, the increase in mechanical strength can be mainly ascribed to the small grain. The WJP treatment mainly affects the surface and subsurface layers.
DOI:10.1007/s11665-022-07155-y

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Steel built-up hollow box columns (HBCs) are widely used in buildings in Asia. The HBC, fabricated by welding four plates to form a built-up box shape, is categorized in the same group as a hollow structural section (HSS) column in AISC 341 . Many cyclic tests have been conducted on these columns, showing that their seismic performance is strongly affected by the member compactness, the amount of axial load, and the material yield strength. However, the AISC 341 , AIJ or Taiwan Code have significantly different width-to-thickness (b/t) requirements for highly ductile box members, leading to different column sections for high seismic application. Table 1 lists formulas and corresponding b/t limits for built-up box columns, calculated based on the nominal yield strength of SM570 steel, Fyn = 420 MPa, the material over-strength factor, Ry = 1.2, and the elastic modulus, E = 200,000 MPa. Note that for highly ductile box members, the b/t requirement in AISC 341 (=12.9) is much lower than 22 or 21.7, calculated based on AIJ or Taiwan Code, respectively.
DOI:10.1016/j.engstruct.2020.111147

问题:Which compound has a ultimate tensile strength of 930 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Fig. 3 and Table 1 compare the tensile properties of hydrogenated-dehydrogenated (HDH) Ti-6Al-4V to simulated cast Ti-6Al-4V and the control experiment (control 675). In each case duplicate tests are shown. Table 1 also compares the tensile properties to the American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM) standards for wrought (ASTM F1472) [] and cast (ASTM F1108-14) [] Ti-6Al-4V. Average test values are quoted in Table 1 to cater for scatter which is up to 20 MPa (for strength) and 1.5% elongation for ductility. Simulated cast Ti-6Al-4V meets the ASTM F1108-14 [] standard for Ti-6Al-4V castings. It has a yield strength (YS) in the order of 760 MPa, an ultimate tensile strength (UTS) in the order of 930 MPa and meets the 10% benchmark ductility. The UTS is comparably higher (930 MPa) than cast Ti-6Al-4V (860 MPa) []. The control 675 sample, on the other hand, exhibits a relative decrease in strength as a result of the extended heat treatment at 650 °C and 675 °C in vacuum but retains full ductility (10%). The HDH treatments for the simulated cast Ti-6Al-4V alloy increases relative strength but deteriorates ductility especially at dehydrogenation temperatures below 750 °C. This is evident after hydrogenating and subsequently dehydrogenating at 675 °C (HDH 675) where significant ductility loss (in the order of 75%) is observed. The lost ductility is recovered to some extent after dehydrogenating above 725 °C and fully restored after dehydrogenating at 750 °C. The work hardening rate of the HDH 750 is slightly higher than the HDH 725 condition and the resultant UTS is approximately 100 MPa higher than the simulated cast condition.
DOI:10.1016/j.matchar.2021.111698

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 637 MPa for TiAl PST?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In conclusion, our study of directionally solidified PST single crystals of TiAl + Nb with different lamellar orientations reveals that it is the anisotropic interfacial energy that controls the final lamellar orientations of these intermetallic crystals. Based on our analyses, we have successfully fabricated PST single crystals with controlled lamellar orientations at a low production cost, without the requirement of complicated and costly crystal-seeding methods. The well-aligned PST single crystals with the 0° lamellar orientation exhibit a combination of a substantially enhanced ductility of 6.3–7.6% and a high strength of 930–1,035 MPa at ambient temperature. The impressive mechanical performance of the present TiAl PST single crystals can be attributed to the formation of high-density nanoscale twins and the refined lamellar structure during plastic deformation. More importantly, the crystals possess a superior yield strength of 637 MPa and a high creep resistance at 900 °C in the stress range 100–210 MPa in air. The creep lifetime and minimum creep rates of our PST single crystals are superior to those of commercial 4822 TiAl polycrystalline material by more than one order of magnitude. Consequently, the service temperature of this crystal material can be potentially raised to as high as 900 °C. Our studies have demonstrated an important advance in the design and fabrication of TiAl + Nb PST single crystals with controlled orientations, and the promising mechanical performance of these alloys indicates that they have significant potential in service environments at higher temperatures.
DOI:10.1038/nmat4677

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):We also measured mechanical properties of Ce-based BMGs. The results were shown in Fig. 5.
